Title: A MASSY "SRK" ACTION THRILLER!
review: Keeping the philosophy of the movie aside for a moment, the movie is a pure Mass action thriller. The opening scene, the pre-interval scene, the bike chase - all of the action sequences were absolutely terrific. Plus the BGM is like cherry on top of the cake. At some points, it also reminded me of money heist. Reason why i say to keep the philosophy aside is because some things portrayed are false. People who don't have basic knowledge of taxation system, the armed forces, NPAs, debt-waiver etc. Will easily get carried away because of the "emotional" factor the movie brings along with these topics. So, watch it only for the action sequences, badass dialogues and BGM. Can't wait for it's sequel because hints are clearly dropped. Love you SRK & Vijay Sethupati sir. And hats off to all the rest of cast & crew!

Title: The hype isn't real
review:
The city where I live they don't release indian/bollywood films in the cinemas. So I had to wait a long time to watch it on the internet.

Apart from the tsunami of hypes, I think this movie had a lot of potential to become a good one. The story had its' perks, the cast did their best to pull off the characters they were given to perform but I think the editing and screenplay ruined it.

As a serial film consumer I don't keep a lot of bollywood movies on my watchlist but since the social media went crazy over this one I had to really spend almost 3 hours to watch this one. And after finishing it I had just one thought in my mind. What went wrong that they couldn't make me feel connected to any characters in 3 hours? I didn't feel joy/sorrow/anger none whatsoever for any characters. They just appear on the screen and some died or went on to do something else. It's all just fun and games and gimmicks of how cool your favorite superstar looks on the screen. And that's IT.

4/10 just for the potential and gimmicks.

Title: Overdone Action, Underwhelming Story
review: This is a film that tries to do too much and ends up doing too little. The plot is derivative, rehashing themes we've seen countless times before without bringing anything new to the table. The heavy-handed sentimentality feels forced, as if the movie is trying to tug at your heartstrings without earning it. And then there's the excessive patriotism, which is laid on so thick it becomes hard to take seriously. The action scenes, while plentiful, often defy logic to the point of absurdity, making it difficult to stay invested. It's a spectacle, sure, but one that's more exhausting than entertaining. If you're looking for substance or originality, "Jawan" might leave you disappointed.

Title: "Jawan (2023): SRK's Masterstroke in Action-Drama"
review:
Once more, the indomitable SRK mesmerizes in "Jawan". From the very first scene, the narrative grips you, pulling you into a whirlwind of suspense so intense it's almost tangible. The film's pulse races in tandem with yours, effortlessly maintaining momentum throughout. Its expertly crafted screenplay ensures fluid transitions between present-day sequences and poignant flashbacks, creating an intricate tapestry that unfolds with seamless finesse.

Every actor in the ensemble cast shines brightly, delivering performances that resonate deeply. Yet, even among such brilliance, the cameos by the iconic Sanjay Dutt and the ever-enigmatic Deepika Padukone stand out, leaving an indelible mark.

But it's not just the performances that captivate. The film's direction is a masterclass in cinematic storytelling. Paired with a haunting background score and razor-sharp editing, it elevates "Jawan" to a league of its own. For aficionados of action and drama, this film isn't just a recommendation-it's an experience, a journey you wouldn't want to miss.

Title: Good SRK Action with lots of flashbacks
review:
Action scenes in this movie especially in the beginning are mind-blowing. Although as the story progresses the series of flashbacks begin which seems acceptable at the start to fill the audience with emotions but when you do it again and again it becomes quite annoying.

Irrespective of all the repetitive history sessions and seemingly slow pace of the story around the interval this movie brings some good action scenes with a perfect touch of comedy. Vijay Sethupati as a villain has portrayed an interesting and at times funny character.

Overall a good movie worth recommending. And if you are an SRK fan then it's a must-watch for you.

Title: Movie is simply engaging and entertaining...
review:
Finally Atlee came with dynamic movie formula for engaging audiences with action pack and blessed producers, distributors with lots of money since it has all commercial elements....
The formula is----->
Flash back + different getup + simple story line + too many emotions + beautiful screen presentation + bold and dynamic music + dark comedy + beautiful heroine + mega villain + baba level surprise actor supoort = block buster Atlee formula......just kidding
But movie is Really magical 2times watchable....
The best part of movie is from starting to ending it is engaging, surprising, with super duper mind blowing si.ple twist and turns.

Title: Slo mo! He don't flinch!
review:
1. Too much slo mo. Every other scene is slo mo, trying too hard to look cool to the point it becomes cringe worthy.

(Case in point, SRK smoking a cigar & doing badass things with plot armor is a child's idea of what looks "badass".) The action scenes would be better if they were fast paced, not slow.

They ruined the potentially good action. People complain about Zack Snyder, wait till you watch this. Even non-action scenes have slo mo.

In a scene, A Wife grabs a piece of cloth to cover her husband's modesty that has been removed by some goons. Even the cloth grabbing scene has slo mo. Unintentionally hilarious.

Sadly indian action cinema is suffering from a slo mo pandemic right now. Get well soon.

2. Bad guys have guns but can't aim. Good guys defeat the villains without guns in atleast some scenes. No respect for the viewers intelligence.

3. Politically preachy. Full on lecture baazi. You're entertainers who we pay to entertain us, not lecture us. There's a way to do it. This film is unsubtle & in your face.

4. Disconnected music videos. Age old problem in Indian cinema. In one of them, a jailer is dancing with his prisoner allies & the films director who makes a cameo.

These add nothing to the film & wastes our time.

SO, whether you should watch it or not? The answer is yes you should give it a try as it is good movie. You can watch it with your family.

Title: Must watch
review: I loved the movie. The storyline, the acting, music, bgm sounds, vfx, everything was amazing. Movie highlighted many social and political issues. It showed the harsh reality of government and it's poor management. Movie gives a very strong message to it's audience that is to stand against evil. Shahrukh khan has proven he is the king of cinema. Vijay as villian once again won our hearts. You will love the acting and cameo role of sanjay dutt. The girls gang was superb. It's a must watch movie. Dialogs were heart woning and SRK delivered them with perfection. His voice changing according to the role was praise worthy.

Title: JAWAN
review:
"Jawan," a gripping action-drama film, delves into themes of corruption, justice, and redemption with a unique and provocative narrative. Directed by a visionary filmmaker, the movie features a prison warden who recruits inmates to carry out audacious crimes designed to expose systemic injustices. The film's intricate plot, dynamic characters, and socially relevant themes create a compelling cinematic experience that resonates deeply with audiences.

At the heart of "Jawan" is the enigmatic prison warden, portrayed by a seasoned actor who brings depth and intensity to the role. The warden is a complex character, driven by a sense of moral duty and a personal vendetta against the corrupt systems that plague society. Disillusioned by the pervasive injustice he witnesses daily, the warden devises a radical plan: to use the skills and desperation of his inmates to commit high-profile crimes that will shine a light on the hidden corruption and injustices that mar the social fabric.

The inmates, each with their own backstories and motivations, form a diverse ensemble that adds layers of complexity to the narrative. From a tech-savvy hacker wrongfully imprisoned to a former military officer seeking redemption, the characters are well-developed and relatable. Their interactions with the warden and with each other create a rich tapestry of relationships that drive the story forward. The film deftly explores their personal struggles and the moral dilemmas they face as they become unwitting pawns in the warden's grand scheme.

The warden's plan unfolds through a series of meticulously orchestrated crimes, each designed to target a specific area of corruption. From exposing corporate malfeasance to highlighting the abuses of political power, the crimes are bold and daring, executed with precision and flair. The film's action sequences are expertly choreographed, blending tension and excitement with a sense of realism that keeps viewers on the edge of their seats. The use of practical effects and stunts, combined with innovative cinematography, enhances the visceral impact of these scenes.

As the warden's campaign of justice gains momentum, it attracts the attention of law enforcement and the media, escalating the stakes and putting the warden and his team in increasingly perilous situations. The film skillfully balances these high-octane moments with quieter, character-driven scenes that explore the psychological and emotional toll of their actions. The warden's motivations and backstory are gradually revealed, adding depth to his character and raising questions about the true nature of justice and the cost of seeking it outside the bounds of the law.

A pivotal aspect of the narrative is the unexpected reunion that the warden experiences as his plan reaches its climax. This reunion serves as a turning point in the story, forcing the warden to confront his past and reevaluate his methods and goals. The emotional weight of this encounter adds a poignant dimension to the film, highlighting the personal stakes involved in the warden's quest for justice.

"Jawan" is not just an action-packed thriller; it is a thought-provoking exploration of societal issues and the lengths to which individuals will go to fight for what they believe is right. The film's commentary on corruption and injustice is timely and relevant, resonating with contemporary audiences who are increasingly aware of these issues in their own lives. The screenplay, marked by sharp dialogue and intricate plotting, keeps viewers engaged and invested in the characters' journeys.

The performances in "Jawan" are uniformly strong, with the lead actor delivering a standout performance that anchors the film. The supporting cast, including the ensemble of inmates, brings authenticity and depth to their roles, making each character memorable and sympathetic. The film's direction is assured, with a clear vision that integrates action, drama, and social commentary into a cohesive and compelling whole.

In conclusion, "Jawan" is a powerful and exhilarating film that transcends genre conventions to deliver a story that is both entertaining and thought-provoking. Its exploration of corruption, justice, and redemption is handled with intelligence and nuance, making it a standout entry in contemporary cinema. Whether you are drawn to its action-packed sequences or its deeper thematic elements, "Jawan" offers a cinematic experience that is sure to leave a lasting impression.

Title: Good
review:
Director Atlee's Bollywood debut, Jawan, is a high-octane, politically charged action spectacle that serves as a magnificent vehicle for the charisma of Shah Rukh Khan. The film is a masterful blend of South Indian 'masala' cinema and contemporary social commentary, resulting in a theatrical experience packed with thrills, emotional depth, and rousing star power.

Khan delivers a powerhouse dual performance as Azad, a prison warden turned righteous vigilante, and the mysterious Vikram Rathore. This dual-role structure fuels a convoluted yet consistently engaging narrative that spans decades and features multiple stunning twists, culminating in a father-son story of vengeance against systemic corruption.

The film's most notable strength is its execution of mass appeal. Featuring meticulously choreographed, gravity-defying action sequences, an infectious score by Anirudh Ravichander, and powerful confrontation scenes with antagonist Kalee (Vijay Sethupathi), Jawan ensures there is never a dull moment over its nearly three-hour runtime. Nayanthara is excellent in her Hindi debut, and a crucial cameo by Deepika Padukone provides the film's emotional core.

While the plot can be overstuffed and occasionally relies on cinematic convenience, the film's sincere effort to address societal issues-including farmer suicides, faulty military equipment, and a lack of medical infrastructure-lends it unexpected weight. It's an unapologetic, larger-than-life blockbuster designed for the crowd, and it breaks box office records by hitting every commercial note with precision and passion.

Title: Looks like Jawan is the Indian version of the series "Money Heist".
review: Jawan is a Bollywood action thriller that follows a group of young patriots who plan to rob the Reserve Bank of India under the guidance of a mysterious mastermind. The movie is clearly inspired by the popular Spanish series Money Heist, which has a similar premise of a heist orchestrated by a genius leader. However, Jawan fails to capture the charm and suspense of the original series, and instead resorts to clichéd dialogues, melodramatic scenes, and unrealistic action sequences. The movie also lacks the depth and complexity of the characters, who are mostly one-dimensional and stereotypical. The only saving grace of the movie is the performance of the lead actor, who manages to infuse some charisma and humor into his role. Jawan is a disappointing attempt to recreate the magic of Money Heist, and does not offer anything new or exciting to the audience.

As for the plot, it seemed like a patchwork of recycled ideas from both older and recent films. Exiting the theater felt like a long-awaited relief, especially given that the headache induced by the noise lingered for nearly a day. The worst part is that I watched this on IMAX. What a waste of money!

Title: Complete package: Several stories with a strong message!!!!
review:
Shah Rukh is ultimately back with this wholesome entertainer, only this time with a strong lessoning.

The opening scene itself made the film engaging and no stopping after that. The most interesting element of the film was its storytelling, I was amazed at how each and every scene unfolded and linked to one another, it reminded me of films like Baahubali that have a beautiful narration.

It is a fiction yet showcases corruption in politics with a few references that are realistic and sometimes even with realistic numbers. Issues like farmers committing suicides, defective weapons on duty, shortage of oxygen cylinders at government hospitals, false criminal charges and crowd funding are shown.

Also, the film is laden with amazing songs- I love the entire album: both the love songs, the dance numbers, the BGM and a variety of SRK looks and each one is amazing, I really liked his bald look and the father's look towards the climax.

There are a plenty of characters in the film other than SRK's double role and looks with amazing performances that will keep you glued to the seat.

The SRK's monologue at the end is a cherry on the cake that urges the people to question the government just like we question every small thing we buy from a store.

Overall, Jawan is ticket to a fun-ride with messaging, drama, action, love and loss: all in one.

Title: A wild and entertaining cinematic SRK experience
review:
The hype was unreal and I entirely bought into it, and I was not disappointed either. JAWAN was a great theatre experience but what surprised me most was how politically charged it was. Just like the tradition of Tamil cinema(it's written and directed by a Tamil filmmaker named Atlee), it focuses on a lot of social issues that range from well-intentioned to horribly misguided. But then again it is to be expected because you can't possibly cram all that much into a 165-minute film without losing nuances.

The film is a celebration of SRK and his undeniable charisma, and he fully got the assignment and the pitch at which he needs to perform. The nonchalant swagger he possesses, especially in the role of Vikram Rathod is awesome. The production design is incredible and the film is breathtakingly shot, inventively edited, well-choreographed, and excellently acted. Action choreography is great and action setpieces are really good. The film also doesn't skimp on hand-to-hand combat sequences as those are also filmed with finesse, although it could have used fewer slo-mo shots.

The musical score by Anirudh is amazing although the songs range from meh to terrible. The film also suffers from an overabundance of forced emotions and many times, it feels manipulated too. The runtime could have been shortened since by the time the film comes to a close and two Shahrukhs are burning the dance floor on
'Not Ramaiya Vastavaiya', you feel a bit exhausted, not because it's boring but due to its sheer length. But these few problems aside, the film was a terrifically engaging affair that fulfils its promise of bringing a full-on entertainer with SRK in a never-before-seen avatar.

Overall, JAWAN was a fantastic cinema experience for me and leaves me hopeful for more such films where two or more industries collaborate to bring out their best respective versions.

Love You King Khan! 💓

Title: Highly Recommended 👍🏼 by Hit Ya Flop - Movie World
review:
It is a Mega Blockbuster again for Shah Rukh Khan. Jawan is undoubtedly a worth-watching mass entertainer on the big screen. The movie is a package of engaging story, entertaining performances, excellent direction, and electrifying larger-than-life action sequences. Director Atlee has given the viewers a rollercoaster ride of emotions with the perfect blend of whistle-worthy dialogues and clap-worthy larger-than-life action scenes.

The writers have done a great job too, the story delivers pertinent messages to society. It touches upon the corruption in the healthcare system, the bad conditions of government hospitals in our country, and the farmer suicides over the non-repayment of bank loans. The gripping screenplay is full of surprises & exciting cameos, and many scenes can leave you with teary eyes. The intermission and finale scenes are the biggest highlights of the film. The stellar star cast of Jawan won't give you any dull moment in its 169-minute long runtime.

This never-before-seen avatar of Shah Rukh Khan keeps you engaged from start to end. He played his both parts perfectly and his character of Vikram Rathore will surely impress the audience. Watching SRK in high-octane action sequences is a treat for his fans. Nayanthara got a decent debut in Bollywood and she manages to leave the mark. Deepika Padukone and Sanjay Dutt's special cameo appearances were praiseworthy.

Vijay Sethupathi deserved better writing for this role, but he never disappoints even with an underwritten role he delivers outstanding performance. Priyamani, Sanya Malhotra, Sanjeeta Bhattacharya, and Lehar Khan were fine in their limited supporting roles. But Sunil Grover and Riddhi Dogra were unable to leave any mark.

Jawan Movie Rating:
Direction 4/5
Acting 3.5/5
Action 4.5/5
Story 4/5
Screenplay 4/5.

Title: Too much meaningless gore and a headless chicken plot
Biggest Hit of Indian Cinema in 2023
review:
A must watch massy film which attack the system.

Note:- This film is all about SRK & his different looks.

SRK has played dual role, namely Vikram Rathod(father) & Azad(son). SRK is just mesmerizing with all of his looks & action scenes.

Deepika Padukone as Aishwarya is wife of Vikram Rathod. Her role is small but quite effective to move the story ahead.

Nayanthara as Narmada Rai is wife of Azad. She's a hijack specialist police officer of Mumbai police. She has done some good action scenes. She looks pretty in romntic scenes & angry in the hijack scenes.

Vijay Sethupathy as Kali is the main antagonist. He has again proved his acting range in various scenes. He also delivers a mesmerizing performance as Kali Gaikwad. He's brutal & mercyless. He doesn't want anyone to oppose him.

Sunil Grover as Irani is effective. Nobody's able to understand his mind unless he unfolds it.

Priyamani(as Lakshmi), Sanya Malhotra(as Eeram), Sanjeeta Bhattacharya(as Helena), Girija Oak(as Ishkra), Lehar Khan(as Kalki) , Aliah Qureishi(as Janvi) are effective in their characters. They look good in their characters.

Riddhi Dogra as Kaveri(Kaveri Amma) looks a bit weird. She's not the correct cast for this role. She looks convincing as young Kaveri. But she as aged Kaveri isn't convincing at all.

The actors playing the friends of Vikram Ratod are convincing in their role.

Sanjay Dutt(as Madhavan Nair) in his cameo role, leaves an impact with his acting.

Music composed by Anirudh Ravichander is attractive. The music doesn't reduce the speed of the film. But it blends with the speed of the film.

Songs like 'Zinda Banda' , 'Chaleya' , 'Faraatta' , 'Not Ramaiya Vastavaiya', 'Jawan title track' leaves a good impct on the film.

BGM is fantastic & gives good support to the film.

Action scenes are very good & looks fresh. This film has raised the bar for the action scenes.

Editing team & VFX team has done a fantastic job. The VFX, CGI all are very neat & doesn't give chance for complaint.

It's a must watch film for all.

Title: Mindless Emotional Attyachaar
review: People may like the movie or not, its their choice but the fact is that the movie stands nowhere near the reasonably good film making standards. Its seemed like an effort to put 100 different plot points to give people an Emotional High but everything defied logic. It is a mass entertainer hoping to ride the tide of uncalled for "Emotional Atyachaar". Hijacking Metro for Loan Waiver, Kidnapping Health Minister for changes in health system, Cop and villain marrying without realizing, then comes Indian Army sub-plot out of the blue and what from the hell else. Emotional scenes are over dramatized to an extent that they can be triggering for some like Suicide scene where no detail is left to the imagination, Children's death scene where they show faces of several children dying. What is happening? It was a proper south Indian masala film that hopes to cash in all the pain points of masses and SRK's star adulation. Every other minute, a masala song comes on screen. I don't understand when you are putting forward such big issues, what is the need for mindless dancing? Plots change so fast that you don't feel any sense of coherence whatsoever. Overall, I felt that it is a khichdi that had better not be served.

Title: Everybody involved in this movie should be jailed for subjecting audiences to the sheer awfulness that is this picture
review:
This is the first time I'm reviewing a movie. Generally, I have a low bar for Hindi movies but this one was so flagrantly awful, stunningly incoherent, emotionally confused, and shamefully crafted that I had to leave this review.

This movie makes no sense. There's not a single redeeming quality: the premise is ridiculous, the acting is comical, the characters and their decisions are bizarre, the underlying narrative is completely incoherent, and the movie sways chaotically from jokey to dramatically tragic. I can't fathom how none of the writers, producers, directors, or actors realized this as they were making this movie.

There is a supposed message of the movie, which quite frankly is shallow and can simply be boiled down to "voting and asking your government" questions is important. No duh, is that supposed to be insightful? Regardless of the supposed "importance" of the message, all the other problems of the movie render it completely inert and hollow.

Please do yourself a favor and vote with your money and don't watch this movie.

Title: Jawan is NOT Pathaan and that's a good thing

Title: "Jawan" - A Three-Hour Yawn Fest
review:
If you're considering watching "Jawan," I'd strongly advise against it. This film, which boasts a runtime of a mind-numbing three hours, left me utterly bored and disappointed. "Jawan" lacks the essential elements that make a movie enjoyable, and there's not a single surprise to be found in its lengthy duration.

To begin with, the movie's pacing is painfully slow. It takes an excruciatingly long time for the plot to develop, and even when it finally does, it fails to capture the audience's interest. The story meanders along, with endless scenes that seem to serve no purpose other than to fill time. It's as if the filmmakers were determined to stretch a thin plot into an epic saga, and the result is a tedious and unengaging narrative.

One of the most frustrating aspects of "Jawan" is its lack of surprise or originality. From start to finish, the story follows a predictable and clichéd path. There are no unexpected twists or turns to keep the audience engaged, and it feels like you've seen this story a hundred times before. The characters are one-dimensional, and their actions and motivations are equally uninspired. It's a classic case of a missed opportunity to bring something fresh and exciting to the screen.

The film's length is also a major issue. At three hours, "Jawan" overstays its welcome by a mile. It's a test of endurance to sit through such a long, uneventful movie, and by the time it finally reaches its underwhelming conclusion, you'll be left wondering why you wasted so much time on it. There are plenty of great films that manage to tell a compelling story in under two hours, so there's no excuse for subjecting the audience to this kind of marathon.

The lackluster performances by the cast don't help matters either. Despite the talent of some of the actors involved, their characters are so poorly written that there's little they can do to salvage the film. It's clear that the script itself is the root of the problem, as even the most seasoned actors can't breathe life into one-dimensional and uninteresting roles.

Additionally, the film's technical aspects, such as cinematography and editing, fail to make up for its narrative shortcomings. There's nothing visually stunning or innovative to grab your attention, and the editing doesn't do much to improve the pacing or flow of the story. It all feels like a missed opportunity to create a visually striking and emotionally resonant film.

In conclusion, "Jawan" is a tedious and uninspired movie that fails to deliver on multiple fronts. Its excruciatingly slow pacing, lack of surprises, and excessive length make it a painful viewing experience. If you value your time and entertainment, I'd recommend giving "Jawan" a pass and seeking out a more engaging and fulfilling cinematic experience elsewhere. There are countless better options out there, and this film simply isn't worth the investment of your time.

Title: Mass at its best with a decent plot
review:
SRK's last mass film Pathaan had several shortcomings, one being a strong story . Jawan manages to resolve this issue and offers even more.

Now once you complete the film, you will realise it's a mix of a few Bollywood films, imo, they are Rowdy Rathore and Satyamev Jayate. Jawan is a mix of these two films. But i will keep the overall plot under wraps in this review but if you have seen those two films, you know what's coming .

The first biggest plus point of this film is the superb presentation of SRK. It's not a spoiler that SRK has played a father son double role and it's evident from the trailer. The presentation of SRK son is pretty good but the presentation of SRK dad, uffffff!!! The crowd went wild seeing SRK dad version for the first time on screen. But then again i have to say that SRK dad is actually a bit inspired by Allu Arjun's AVPL style. His dialogue delivery is just terrific!!

Next are the mass action sequences. The biggest bombastic sequence was the one just before interval. Absolutely that sequence made the crowd go mad with cheering and hooting. Next in line is the highway action sequence, it's very well shot and keeps you entertained in that entire sequence. Many others are there, all are quite thrilling to witness but the ones i mentioned are the best ones.

Screenplay I would say sometimes drops here and there but mostly is fast paced. Wastes no time in taking off.

The film atleast has a good message to deliver to us . It has a lot of emotional moments especially in the second half and it works pretty well.

Performances are quite good although I felt Sunil Grover was a bit wasted . This is a female oriented film with SRK at the center so the female leads like Nayanthara, Sanya, Priyamani etc have done a very good job.

Negatives - Vijay Sethupathi!! His character neither has good dialogues and neither does his character have depth. His character is cliched and felt like makers spent only a few minutes to write his character. Songs are mid. Also one thing I notice in south director films especially the mass ones is that they don't allow many scenes to settle , it's like they want to move to the next scene in a flash of a second. Jawan too falls prey to this .

Two cameos are there in the film, one which is already known and the other is hidden for you to go watch the film and discover. The known one has a sort of a long cameo actually.

Overall, a highly enjoyable film that presents SRK in his massiest avatar ever and you don't want to miss it in theatre .

Title: "Jawan 2023: A Disastrous Disappointment"
review:
"Jawan 2023" is a cinematic catastrophe, an utter letdown that should serve as a cautionary tale for filmmakers and audiences alike. This movie, in all its glory, exemplifies the worst aspects of modern cinema.

From the very beginning, it's clear that "Jawan 2023" lacks any semblance of a coherent plot. The storyline meanders aimlessly, jumping from one disjointed scene to another, leaving the viewer utterly baffled. It's as if the script was an afterthought, hastily cobbled together without any regard for narrative structure or logic.

The characters are so one-dimensional that they make cardboard cutouts seem complex. They lack depth, motivation, or any reason for the audience to care about their fate. Even the talented cast can't salvage this sinking ship with their uninspired performances.

The action sequences, if they can even be called that, are a cringe-worthy display of incompetence. It's a cacophony of poorly choreographed fights and explosions that only serve to numb the senses rather than entertain. It's clear that no effort was made to make these scenes engaging or memorable.

The dialogue in "Jawan 2023" is a painful assault on the ears. It's filled with cringe-inducing one-liners and melodrama that wouldn't pass muster in a high school play. The attempts at humor are embarrassingly flat, and the emotional moments are so contrived that they elicit more laughter than tears.

The technical aspects of the film are equally abysmal. The cinematography is uninspired, the editing is jarring, and the music feels out of place in almost every scene.

In summary, "Jawan 2023" is a disaster of epic proportions. It's a film that should be studied as an example of everything that can go wrong in filmmaking. There's nothing redeeming about it, and it's a painful experience for anyone unfortunate enough to watch it. Save your time and your sanity and steer clear of this cinematic catastrophe.

Atlee has done a mind-blowing job.

Movie is going to break all the records of Indian Cinema.

1 time watching is not enough need to watch it atleast 2 times more
4.5 out of 5 for Jawan movie
A very good movie with a great message.

Title: Gabbar is Back + Aakhiri Raasta = Undercooked Jawan
review:
The theme of Jawan is based on a "revolution" where oppressed souls form a lethal team and revolts against the corrupt system of the country.

Jawan has its moments, but the film is average with over the top action sequences, Atlee missed the trick, If he have stick towards the drama tone, the film could have been the best film in the history of Indian Cinema, as he had Shahrukh Khan but he opted to choose the route of mass action Entertainer, which is a total miss with me as I had already watched Gabbar is Back with a little bit flavour of Aakhiri Raasta.

Coming to the hysteria which Shahrukh Khan created, I find him exaggerating the Pathan phenomenon, his dialogue delivery is same with the tone of voice which irked me to the core, a deep throat to make it like aggressive. He had two characters in the film which he nailed in some parts and totally flat in other especially with Azad.

Vikram Rathore was force to reckon, but I was thinking that If Salman had done the same, he would be trolled with all the "Bhai - Verse" memes but as he is Shahrukh Khan, he is spared.

Action Choreography is good but not that spectacular as it is presented to be expected. Music kills the momentum of the film & Vijay Sethupati was under utlulized.

Don't expect anything spectacular and you'll enjoy the film.

Title: Joyous Jawan
review: If you are new to slow motion cuts, then this movie is a good introduction. Shah Rukh Khan looks so very charming in this movie and also has a badass double role version. Story is predictable but the screenplay is worth watching with some great action scenes and good little comedies here and there. Songs are very well placed and Deepika's part will emotionally melt everyone. Vijay Sethupathi role was not very impactful like the Master movie if you have watched it. Other female characters also had a considerable screen time which was nice to watch. Overall if you are a person who watches Indian cinema with a enjoying mindset then this movie is for you!

Title: Inconsistent screenplay & Disappointing story
review:
Watched the movie with great enthusiasm and expectation from the story. It started with a momentum, but it lost its value. Then I found this movie is an absolute mix of Tamil movies like "anniyan" the story as well as the mask, Mersal, Vikram, Bigil, and Money Heist (N-Series). The story is inconsistent with many irritating segments.

I'm wondering how could one say this is a Hollywood-level movie, Hollywood movies known for their screenplay which glues us to the seats. Also, the story will be subconsciously acceptable with the details. I don't find any of the Hollywood qualities in this movie.

Title: King takes the throne now.
review:
Jawan is biggest monstrous in 2023. Movie is absolutely electrifying in story, acting, social message, fill so bigger and large scale movie.

SRK is new mass entertainer now.

Basic Plot is A vigilante and his crew take on corrupt institutions in bed with a deadly arms dealer as past and present thrillingly collide.

The action in Jawan is as slickly persuasive as you'd expect from a big-budget Atlee film. Drones, choppers, gatling guns; the director raids the whole inventory of Hollywood-style action blockbusters. However, what really sells these set pieces, besides some fluid jumps and kicks from Khan, are the smudges of Indianness applied to the grand canvas. I loved the idea of one of the hijackers leaving the crime scene in an auto.

So, we're all set to witness history being rewritten, and it wouldn't be an overstatement to say that the film will do business in the range of 600-625 crores net in India.

Title: Typical boring movie
review:
It is totally boring movie, no story, typical south Indian movie. Watching this movie was a torture of 3 hours. I just enjoyed AC and popcorn and nothing else.

Fact is that the movie stands nowhere near the reasonably good film making standards. Its seemed like an effort to put 100 different plot points to give people an Emotional High but everything defied logic. It is a mass entertainer hoping to ride the tide of uncalled for "Emotional Atyachaar". Hijacking Metro for Loan Waiver, Kidnapping Health Minister for changes in health system, Cop and villain marrying without realizing, then comes Indian Army sub-plot out of the blue and what from the hell else. Emotional scenes are over dramatized to an extent that they can be triggering for some like Suicide scene where no detail is left to the imagination, Children's death scene where they show faces of several children dying. What is happening? It was a proper south Indian masala film that hopes to cash in all the pain points of masses and SRK's star adulation. Every other minute, a masala song comes on screen. I don't understand when you are putting forward such big issues, what is the need for mindless dancing? Plots change so fast that you don't feel any sense of coherence whatsoever. Overall, I felt that it is a khichdi that had better not be served.

Title: You should watch this movie I'm not a fan of srk at all but this time he is a true jawan ...the moral is great about politics in India , the story line is kicking, srk vs Vija
Hats Off! Best Flim of the Century!!
review: "Jawan" is a cinematic masterpiece that seamlessly blends heart-pounding action, emotional depth, and a powerful narrative that grips the audience from the very beginning to the closing credits. Directed by the talented and visionary filmmaker, this film is a shining example of what Indian cinema can achieve when it marries impeccable storytelling with stellar performances. After watching this movie. I've felt like its the best movie bollywood industry ever delivered. Jawan movie brought me back to fall in love with bollywood again. What a movie and message also visuals are amazing. We're hoping bollywood will deliver more meaningful movie again and again like this one. Recommended to everyone!

Title: A must watch movie
review: Wonderful performance by SRK. His versatility and different looks was one of the highlights of the movie. It is a best masala family entertainer. Atlee's direction surprised me. I also liked the chemistry between SRK and Nayanthara. Deepika Padukone's role was very strong and she beautifully portrayed her role. Vijay Setupati's role was okay, i think there was a little scope to work on his role, could have been better. All the supporting actors did very good job. I liked the background music, complemented the action scenes very well. Overall loved the movie. Action sequences was too good. I will watch it again.

Title: Pathetic movie
review: Even from trailer it looks pathetic, wonder why people are so much delighted to go and watch in theatre. Same copy content from marvel and south. Seems like a platter served and nothing original. Sharukh over smart acting makes it worst same which was seen in Pathan. Songs are even worse than movie. Saw one review where one compared the idea from Amitabh's Akhiri Rasta and it seems correct. Actions scenes are itself which will make you recall as if watching typical south movie. It's shocking how people survived the entire 3 hours in theatre and then coming out safe without having headache. Better to watch in OTT even if you dare, at least you can forward or switch it off.

Title: Best movie of 2023 and Srk at his best
review:
One of the best performance by Shah Rukh Khan. Story line is not exceptional but treatment is outstanding.

Songs are good and gets blend with the story. Background music is outstanding which adds more flavour.

Action sequences are well choreographed and VFX were also international standards.

This movie will be considered as SRK's one of the best movie in terms of box office collection and also for the quality of the movie.

Vijay setupathi in negative character is always treat to watch and no exception in this case too.

Overall: An visual delight which is must watch for all the movie lovers

10/10.

Title: It's not a movie it's really imotion
review:

Title: GO & WATCH

Title: Excellent Movie with superb direction...!!
review:
Love this Movie...The storyline is so intriguing which can't allow the audience to blink eye during running time.

The message was clear and delivered. Second time after bahubali, I saw people shouting, clapping and cheering for the hero massy avatar and even cherish his presence on screen and every time a social message delivered it was accepted with claps and shouting.

This movie really makes you think from the core to raise the voice with the real social aspects without losing the story grip.

You will love and cherish SRK presence in massy avatar...go watch it and you will never disappoint.

Title: Disappointing for a South Indian directed movie
Charm and versatility of SRK with a deep message that will impact your perspective as a citizen.
review:
Action and political corruption collide in this gripping cinematic thriller. Jawan's intense action sequences keep your heart racing, while its portrayal of political intrigue adds depth to the plot. With a well-paced narrative and strong performances, it's a compelling exploration of power and its corrupting influence. A must-see for those who enjoy adrenaline-pumping action with a side of political drama.

The only average aspect is the songs of the movie, which I felt it lacked to pull the song loving audience but if you'll watch the movie in the theatre you'll enjoy the songs as well and may feel the urge to grove with your most loving SRK and his girls.

Title: Super Flop Movie
review:
It's a Super Flop Movie, waste of time and money. No innovation and nothing new, it's very much over - hyped.

If Bollywood wants to grow and gain more worldwide recognition, they can't just focus on a handful of people and their lineage anymore. They need to focus on hard hitting scripts, actors playing age appropriate roles.. basic things that have been neglected because having a Khan on the poster was always a guarantee to producers of getting a return back.

I adore ShahRukh and Aamir and I look forward to seeing their releases everytime I hear an announcement, but the end of the Khan era is long overdue. The talent in the current generation is of an exceptional level and will take the industry to new heights.

To be really honest - I didnt like it.

SRK is no more the king of Bollywood.

Title: Jawan: A Force for Good
review:
The film follows the story of a man who is set to rectify the wrongs in the society.

The film opens with a bang, with a thrilling action sequence involving Shah Rukh Khan. Khan is in top form, and he brings his trademark charisma and humor to the role. Vijay Sethupathi is also excellent as the villain, and he has some great chemistry with Khan. The supporting cast is also good, with Nayanthara, Sanya Malhotra, Priyamani, Sunil Grover, and Sanjay Dutt all giving solid performances.

The action sequences in Jawan are some of the best I've seen in a Bollywood film in recent years. They are well-choreographed and exciting, and they never feel over-the-top. The film also has a strong emotional core, and it deals with some important social issues.

Overall, Jawan is a satisfying and engaging action thriller. It is a must-see for fans of Shah Rukh Khan and Atlee.

Some of the things I liked about the film:

The action sequences are top-notch. Shah Rukh Khan is in top form. Vijay Sethupathi is excellent as the villain. The supporting cast is good. The film deals with some important social issues.

Some of the things I didn't like about the film:

The film is a bit overlong. The plot is a bit convoluted. Some of the dialogue is cheesy.

Overall, I enjoyed Jawan. It is a well-made action thriller with strong performances from the cast. I would recommend it to fans of Shah Rukh Khan and Atlee.

Here are some of the things that I think could have been improved:

The film could have been trimmed down by about 30 minutes. The plot could have been simplified a bit. Some of the dialogue could have been better.

Despite these flaws, I still enjoyed Jawan. It is a fun and exciting action thriller with a good message. I would recommend it to fans of Shah Rukh Khan and Atlee.

Title: Absolutely Massy Massy Massy Massy treat
review: Watched the movie on the first day first show with my friends and second day last show with my father and I can truly say this is the best mass movie of Hindi film industry if not Indian cinema. The theatre experience was best surpassing my previous best theatre experience ie Raees. I screamed my lungs out but my I wanted to scream even more. And the visual effects and background music was phenomenal. If you want to watch the movie watch it in a single screen theatre if you can multiplexes are good but they can never match the theatre experience of a single screen theatre. Man I cannot express how happy I am as a SRKian. And love you Atlee sir from bottom of my heart (Marana Maasss). Vijay Sethupathi (Attakasamana) what a dream come true for me watching him play a baddie against SRK (Santa is here for you and he has a gift for you) . Anirudh Sirr your BGM was massy massy massyyyyyy you could've done better with the music but it was good ( still want the BGM of each characters like vikram ). Yogi Babu was missing in the Hindi version.

Title: SRK stardom at its very best!!
review: The most anticipated movie of the year is here and SRK singlehandedly steal the show. It starts with a beautiful green scene and water where some villagers are working and they found a body in water which is SRK. They brought him home where SRK is cured. After the initial fight scene the movie goes "after 30 years" and now the story explores and it just keeps you on the edge with its fast pace which is very good thing to be honest it does not give audience a chance where they can feel boredom. Title: Ignore the negative reviews
review: This is is a great action movie by SRK. Full entertainment. Ignore the negative reviews. Overall very good performance by all actors, good direction and average story line. The message is strong especially SRKS monologue is amazing. Don't go in expecting logic in everything. Just go in with no expectations and you won't be disappointed. The music is great. Choreography is good. Story is gripping, especially the 1st half. 2nd half holds up well too. Good movie by SRK after quite sometime and it was refreshing compared to other Bollywood movies nowadays. It's a great family entertainer. Will be going for a 2nd show this weekend!

Title: Blessed to watch SRK perform!!
review:

2. Its a full massy film, it has every flavour a cinema needs. And as a bonus, this one has a good story, a plot, kinda rare these days! Title: Ultimate Mass Masala Entertainment Blockbuster!!
review:
I was genuinely impressed by this Ultimate Mass Masala Entertainer. Shahrukh Khan was nothing short of phenomenal, showcasing acting that was both powerful and nuanced. Nayantara was equally amazing, and their on-screen chemistry was palpable. It was a delight to see them share the screen.

The supporting actors, such as Priyamani and Sanya Malhotra, held their own, adding layers to the story and enhancing each scene they were a part of.

All in all, the movie was a well-crafted mix of emotion, action, and pure entertainment. Definitely a worthwhile experience.

Title: So bad, no connection with the movie
review: Not sure what people liked in it. There is no connection either with story or character. Just like a YouTube video which keeps running in background, this movie runs same way while watching. Story is jumping here and there, so many iconic scenes are taken from other movies. I think people just went to watch srk with any other hero this would have been a flop. Mummy taken from moon knight , banes costume is taken. Srk characters looks like have multiple personality disorder which keeps on changing every 10mins. No consistency in characters. Sometime they are showing as if they are top commando and next moment crying like a baby with little pain.

Title: Must watch movie in 2023
review:
Jawan, the latest cinematic offering, is a compelling rollercoaster of emotions, anchored by Shahrukh Khan's stellar performance. The film seamlessly blends action, and suspense, delivering an unforgettable cinematic experience. Shahrukh Khan, in his dual roles, shines brightly on the silver screen. Shahrukh Khan effortlessly switches between the dedicated and disciplined soldier, showcasing his versatility as an actor.

The storyline of Jawan is a gripping narrative that keeps you on the edge of your seat from start to finish. It explores themes of duty, sacrifice, and betrayal within the backdrop of a high-stakes military operation. The film's twists and turns are expertly woven into the plot, ensuring that the audience is in for a thrilling ride.

The supporting cast also deserves applause for their performances, adding depth and authenticity to the story. The action sequences are breathtaking, and the cinematography beautifully captures the scenic locations and intense moments.

Title: JAWAN is a movie of 1.40 billion People
review:
Shah Rukh Khan has always been vocal, opinionated and honest in real life. But over the past decade, people have questioned his silence. Even when faced with the toughest phases in life, he has always maintained a poised stance, dignified and respectful. Jawan seems like the perfect outlet for him to let out his war cry. It is a tight slap across the faces of all the nay sayers. He is like a raging lion carefully protecting his family and country. I can't help but feel his "Betey Ko haath lagane pehle baap se baat kar" is aimed at all those scamsters that wrongfully framed his son.

To see him soar to new heights as an actor after having achieved superstardom that no one has quite seen and I doubt someone will ever see, is a thrill! To see him just slip into these roles with so much swag and ease is just sheer delight. Atlee has taken SRK to a zenith with this movie. It is a well made, well executed mass movie with plenty of messaging. And necessary messaging given the upcoming 2024 election. There are instances that have been directly lifted from real life incidences and the truth delivered. No wonder why already Boycott Jawan is trending on Twitter, sorry X.

It is a fantastic movie with a lot of soul. I cried inconsolably, laughed, clapped and smiled with pride. Terrific supporting cast and a superlative Vijay Sethupathi. The background music is brilliant! It's refreshing to see some big names down south in this movie. Nayanthara is effortless and looks good as the newest pair with Shah. Special thanks to him for bringing out this side in Shah and to let him chase his wishlist of being an action star 🌟

Watch the movie if you want some "meaningful cinema". Watch the movie if you want entertainment. And above all watch the movie if you are not a SRK fan. You will realize why half the world worships.

Title: Celebration of King Khan
review:
It's an unapologetic celebration of the myth, the man, the legend that is SRK. Atlee not only shines as a director in his ability to present SRK in his 'mass-iest' avatar yet, but also in his knowledge of how to use colour,his sense of pacing a sequence kudos to some masterful editing and where to put his actors in a given frame, some topnotch cinematography.

Though having exhilarating action sequences with some applause worthy VFX work, some strong, repetitive but neccesary social commentary, the heart and soul of this film has been kept grounded in its script - the films strongest yet most problematic part, let's talk about it.

There is an attempt to fit a web-series worth of story in a movie, which results in the people behind the camera having to juggle it's characters, handpicking ones to give love, resulting in others getting absolutely nothing.

There are moments when the script demands to sit with the charecters in a moment of silence, but it just moves right forward which results in the point being made, but not hammered home. Easy way to fix this? Take out the 25 mins worth of songs and use those valuable 25 mins to build up your charecters.

Credit to the filmmakers, they are doing their best to accomodate the story in the given runtime and the decisions, the shortcuts, the sacrifices that were made are understandable, and hey we are here to see SRK and it more than delivers.

Title: "Jawan: A Perilous Plunge into Mediocrity"
review:
Jawan, a movie that promised to be action-packed, gripping, and entertaining, instead manages to disappoint on all fronts. From its lackluster screenplay to its uninspired direction, Jawan proves to be an exhausting viewing experience that fails to engage or captivate its audience.

The film revolves around a military officer, played by a miscast and unconvincing lead actor, who is tasked with thwarting an impending terrorist attack. Unfortunately, the story lacks depth and substance, relying heavily on tired clichés and predictable plot twists that offer little surprise or intrigue. The movie's attempts at showcasing patriotism and heroism fall flat, leaving the audience emotionally detached and uninvested.

One of the biggest flaws of Jawan lies in its pacing. The film moves aimlessly, with no clear direction or purpose, leading to a disjointed narrative that fails to establish a strong connection between the characters and their motivations. The poorly written dialogue only adds to the film's woes, offering cringe-worthy lines that make it difficult to take the movie seriously.

Even the action sequences, which one would expect to be the saving grace of a film like this, are lackluster and poorly executed. The choreography feels lazy and uninspired, failing to deliver the high-octane thrills that one would hope for. This, coupled with the film's subpar visual effects, further contributes to the overall disappointment.

Another glaring issue with Jawan is its underutilization of its talented supporting cast. While the film boasts a talented ensemble, their talents are squandered in poorly written and uninteresting roles, leaving the audience craving more depth and development from the characters.

In the end, Jawan is an unfortunate misfire that falls victim to its own lack of ambition and creativity. With its weak screenplay, unconvincing performances, and uninspired direction, it becomes clear that this film had potential that was ultimately wasted. Save your time and seek out better options in the genre, rather than subjecting yourself to this forgettable and lackluster experience.

Title: King Khan's feast delivered in typical Atlee style
review:
Some movies don't tease us with new concepts, rather serves simple recipes, in the most explosive manner. Jawan, just like Pathaan, belongs to the latter category, although in a more massier way.

Shah Rukh Khan's screen presence is magnetic, he makes you hoot and cheer, even if you aren't his ardent fan. No doubt, he's one of the biggest crowd puller in Indian cinema.

Vijay Sethupathi, as usual, plays the antagonist role in his own style, with a tinge of fun angle. Nayanthara looks stylish, but she surprised with her agility in stunts, amazing. Deepika's got a predictable role, yet leaves a mark.

Technically, cinematographer GK Vishnu deserves thunderous applause, really enhanced the scale of the film through his work, slick, stylish and superior. Editing by Ruben is smooth, narrative doesn't feel rushed. Stunts are designed well, especially the interval and truck chase. Production values and VFX of Red Chillies is superb. Anirudh's songs are just good, but he makes it up with BGM, keeping the proceedings alive.

Writing wise, it's a simple solid straightforward story, but screenplay could have been better, especially in the second half. Dialogues by Sumit Arora are whistle-worthy.

Coming to Atlee, as a writer, he did an okayish job, but his direction is top-class, presenting SRK in the most massiest avatar fans could ever imagine. There's a fine line between mass and cringe, he's one of those rare directors who knows that and delivers blockbusters.

Bottom-line: If Pathaan was mass, Jawan is Baap of that.

Title: Excellent acting by tamil stars! Tamizha Zindabad!!!
review:
Everything good apart from the the fact that SRK, the only one from bollywood and the only reason to release a tamil movie as bollywood movie. Fans would have been much more happier if this is a kollywood movie as per the stars excluding SRK. We could include him as it can be a debut for him but they wanted to do it the other way around.

Apart from that movie is a typical south indian movie, for which bollywood audience could apparently get super excited but not a great one here down. Anyhow thanks for taking time and showing our beloved stars fron the south to all over India and across the world. Tamizha zindabad!

Title: Made an account just so I can write an honest review!
review:
I am a huge SRK die hard fan since I was 6 years old. I am now 32 for context.

The last few movies he made were an absolute flop. I was so let down lately I wasn't going to bother watching this one. HOWEVER Jawan went above and beyond my expectations!

DO NOT HESITATE WATCHING! YOU MUST watch it on the big screen, at home won't do it justice.

It had subtle political messages and shah rukh absolutely nailed the action. So many emotions when I watched it, srk is back! It makes me sad that he is the last of the stars. I hope he keeps releasing movies like this, I will honestly be heart broken when he no longer comes as an action hero.

Title: Not being a Shahrukh Fan, I still loves it!!
review:
Being not a Shahrukh fan I still loved the movie!! The action, story, actors, everything is just awesome about this movie. Shahrukh khan sets another milestone with this one. His age does not reflect at all in his character.

Vijay Setupathi does total justice to his role. Nayanthara is a true beauty and shines in her role.

About the story, Personally I think if something like Jawan happens in real life, our country would be a better place to survive, the story is well executed by the director.

More missions pertaining to other girls would have been best to be seen in action and I sense there would be a sequel coming up with that thought!!

The songs could have been avoided to reduce the length but its bollywood so we cant help it!!

Its a must watch definately!!

Title: Too many unnecessary songs make the plot a bit dull, but SRK stardom was off the charts
review:
The storyline is quite decent, and the important messages sent to the mass people throughout the entire film made it better. However, at some points the plot seemed rushed and low-depth - arising several plot-holes in my opinion. One more thing what irritated me was the excessive use of songs every now and then, which felt totally unnecessary. Well, given that it's an Indian cinema made for mass people - a few songs won't hurt but there's just too many.

Factoring all those issues in, this film should've gotten a 7 at best.

But. The SRK effect. His performance and aura alone were sufficient to make this film thoroughly entertaining, and the lead villain role as well as the supporting actors did pretty well too. The action sequences combined with good background music were enjoyable, although they were... very... Tamil. Nonetheless, like I said, enjoyable. Not to mention a decent use of humor as well. A combination of all these manages to deliver a good theatre experience to the public - hence, an 8 - a tribute to the last of the stars!

Title: Pathetic Direction and Story Telling
review:
Too many characters and no justification of their roles, story telling is very weird. 2nd half has no sense.

Too much focus on SRK after having more than 20 characters in the movie.

Villain introduction is not there. Vijay sethupati could have do
ne a lot more in this movie but ATLEE has no sense of movie making.

Title: Jawan - A Cinematic Triumph Blending Entertainment and Substance
review:
"Jawan" stands as an exemplar of cinematic excellence in the realm of full-on entertainment. Shah Rukh Khan (SRK), the undisputed king of Bollywood, once again graces the silver screen with his inimitable charisma and talent, reaffirming his timeless reign in the industry. After a career spanning over three decades, SRK's allure remains unparalleled, a testament to his enduring appeal.

One of the most striking facets of "Jawan" is SRK's commanding presence. His portrayal of the protagonist is nothing short of a tour de force, reminding audiences why he remains the quintessential Bollywood superstar. His nuanced performance, even after three decades in the industry, showcases an actor at the peak of his prowess. His ability to seamlessly transition between emotions, from heartwarming moments to intense action sequences, is a testament to his versatility and experience.

The film's visual effects (VFX) deserve special mention. In an era where audiences are increasingly discerning about the quality of VFX, "Jawan" raises the bar with its stunning visual spectacle. The VFX team has meticulously crafted every frame, seamlessly integrating CGI elements into the narrative. From high-octane action sequences to breathtaking scenic backdrops, "Jawan" immerses viewers in a world where the line between reality and fantasy blurs. The result is a visually captivating experience that enhances the overall cinematic journey.

At the heart of "Jawan" lies a compelling and thought-provoking story. It's not just a superficial action-packed extravaganza; it has depth and substance that resonate with audiences on multiple levels. The narrative delves into themes of resilience, determination, and the indomitable spirit of the human soul. It explores the complexities of the protagonist's journey, making viewers reflect on their own lives and aspirations.

The action sequences in "Jawan" are nothing short of breathtaking. The choreography, executed with precision, keeps viewers on the edge of their seats throughout the film. Each fight scene is a visual spectacle, a testament to the meticulous planning and execution by the action team. SRK's physicality and commitment to his role shine through in every punch and kick, further solidifying his position as the ultimate action hero.

While "Jawan" is undeniably a rollercoaster of entertainment, it also imparts valuable life lessons. Beneath the surface of its action-packed exterior lies a story of perseverance and the pursuit of one's dreams. It inspires viewers to overcome obstacles and challenges with unwavering determination, a message that resonates with people from all walks of life.

In conclusion, "Jawan" is a masterclass in Bollywood filmmaking, a movie that seamlessly blends entertainment with substance. SRK's performance is a testament to his enduring star power, while the outstanding VFX, gripping story, and adrenaline-pumping action sequences ensure an enjoyable cinematic experience. Moreover, the film's ability to convey meaningful life lessons elevates it beyond mere entertainment.

"Jawan" not only entertains but also leaves a lasting impact, encouraging viewers to chase their dreams with unwavering determination. It is a cinematic triumph that will undoubtedly be remembered as one of the best movies of our times, a testament to the timeless appeal of Shah Rukh Khan and the magic of Bollywood. This movie is, in a word, "massyyyy" in every sense of the word.

Title: Kichadi without pressure cooker

Started off well, keeping the audience engaged, but the more i watched the movie, my iq kept dropping.

There are just too many subplots to keep track off, and directors think having long action sequences with loud music in the background is what makes a movie a hit.

Personally, none of the side characters performed any well, you just went to see the movie for SRK.

I think the hype and love for the actor has caused people to just be overexcited by his screen precess, and they will write and applaud the movie like it's one of the best things they have watched.

I left without watching the ending, but the film after intermission is just mind-numbing. Furthermore, I would rather watch any old Hindi movie that has must better emotions, acting and story line.

Rocky prem kahani is a much better watch, despite being pretty bad itself.

Also, the movie has too many dark themes, which is not advisable to watch for a "pg13" movie.

SRK will continue to present such boring films because he has mastered this formula for a hit movie:

Loyal fan base + add south elements to the movie + action sequences which we have seen a million times and release the movie in 8 different languages to really collect that box office numbers.

I personally think Raees was srk's last good movie and one should skip this, honestly.

Rather watch Billu barber if one wants to enjoy SRK screen presence.

Title: Boring at times too many songs
review:
The film "Jawan" appears to be a blend of some previous ones, with significant elements seemingly borrowed from the popular series "Money Heist." Unfortunately, this results in a rather underwhelming cinematic experience that ultimately feels like a waste of good three hours.

One of the most striking aspects of "Jawan" is its apparent resemblance to "Money Heist." From the intricate heist plot to the quirky and often morally ambiguous characters, it's hard to ignore the parallels between the two. While inspiration from successful works can be a creative starting point, "Jawan" struggles to carve out its unique identity, leaving audiences with a sense of déjà vu.

The central plot of "Jawan" revolves around a high-stakes heist, where a group of individuals with specialized skills comes together to pull off an audacious robbery. Sound familiar? It should, because this premise has been explored in various forms in countless heist movies and TV series. While there's nothing inherently wrong with revisiting a tried-and-true formula, "Jawan" fails to inject freshness into the narrative. Instead, it relies heavily on borrowed ideas, leading to a lack of genuine excitement or surprise.

The characters in "Jawan" also bear a striking resemblance to those from "Money Heist." The leader of the group is a charismatic and enigmatic figure who goes by the alias "The Mastermind." This character archetype is not only reminiscent of the Professor from "Money Heist" but also lacks the depth and complexity that made the Professor such a captivating character. Similarly, the other members of the team, each with their unique skill set, feel like carbon copies of characters we've seen before in similar heist-themed stories.

One of the most disappointing aspects of "Jawan" is its failure to capitalize on the potential of its talented cast. While the actors give competent performances, they are let down by a lackluster script that doesn't allow them to shine. The character development is minimal, leaving viewers with little reason to invest emotionally in their fates. This lack of connection with the characters makes it difficult to become truly engrossed in the unfolding heist drama.

Furthermore, "Jawan" suffers from pacing issues that make its three-hour runtime feel excessive. The film often meanders through subplots and unnecessary scenes, stretching the narrative thin and testing the patience of the audience. In contrast, "Money Heist" managed to maintain a taut and gripping narrative throughout its episodes, keeping viewers on the edge of their seats.

While "Jawan" attempts to replicate the high-stakes tension of "Money Heist," it falls short in terms of execution. The heist sequences lack the meticulous planning and strategic brilliance that made "Money Heist" so engaging. Instead, they come across as contrived and unconvincing, undermining the film's credibility.

Visually, "Jawan" has its moments, with some well-choreographed action sequences and stylish cinematography. However, these moments are few and far between, and they are not enough to salvage the overall viewing experience. The film's production values are solid, but they can't compensate for its lack of originality and narrative depth.

In conclusion, "Jawan" is a disappointing attempt to capitalize on the success of "Money Heist." While it borrows elements from the popular series, it fails to deliver a compelling and original narrative. The characters lack depth, the pacing is uneven, and the heist sequences lack the intricacy and tension that made "Money Heist" a global phenomenon. Ultimately, "Jawan" feels like a missed opportunity and a waste of good three hours for both fans of heist genres and general moviegoers.

Title: Waste of resources - Let me explain!
review:
I agree that the issues this movie wanted the audience to pay attention to need to be talked about and addressed. What I do not understand about Bollywood, and by extension SRK, is why they make such masala movie given that they are perfectly capable of, and equipped to create a sensible cinema. Mindless action, too much of slo-mo, unrealistic events, unnecessary songs (terrible ones), louder background score is not the answer. Same issues can be portrayed in a sensible manner, take Article 15 as an example. Not only that, the choppiness of the movie really put me off and it made it hard to emotionally connect to the social issues portrayed by the movie. If you want the people to believe in the cause, make the movie believable! Unfortunately, these masala blends work with the audience and this blocks the way for meaningful cinema as the makers are probably scared of the losses. And then, we complain that Bollywood don't win on bigger international stage.

IMO, a colossal waste of talent and resources.

Title: Bombastic is the only word to describe JAWAN
review:
After hearing a lot of good reviews and the ultimate craze for this movie I decided to hop on the train as well and check it out. I'll admit that I wasn't a very big fan of Pathaan so I was kinda anxious about Jawan as well but I was proven wrong during the first 5 minutes. SRK's grand entry was so strong and intense that my jaw dropped to the floor. The action, the background score, the direction, the atmosphere everything was intense and bombastic.

Speaking of the performances, this is SRK's movie and he chews up his dual role of Vikram Rathore and Azad Rathore. Aside from SRK Nayanthara as Narmada was easily the best part about the movie. Vijay Sethupathi as Kalie was great and Deepika Padukone with the limited amount of screentime she had delivered a heartbreaking performance as Aishwarya. There's one more suprise cameo towards the end of the movie which I'm not going to spoil for anyone.

The story was very well-written and despite being nearly 3 hours long the movie didn't dragged or got boring even for a second thanks to its faced paced screenplay. The subject matter is really strong and depicts the faults in our political system with some really gut-wrenching scenes. On top of the that the portrayal of women empowerment in this movie is amazing. However the musical numbers aren't as great with Zinda Banda being the only one song that's catchy enough but the rest are pretty forgettable.

Overall, Jawan is a goddamn spectacle that celebrates the grand return of King Khan (Aside from Pathaan ofcourse). The action is fantastic, the story is great and the social commentary is spot on. Atlee has created something really special.

Title: JAWAN:-if u plan to watch it, watch it in theatres
Jawan: A Disappointing Iteration of Familiar Tropes
review:
"Jawan" is a film that struggles to break free from the shadow of its predecessors, offering little in terms of originality or engaging content. The storyline feels like a tired rehash of familiar concepts, leaving audiences with a sense of déjà vu rather than intrigue.

One of the most glaring issues with "Jawan" is its lackluster acting. Even with the inclusion of a seasoned actor like Shah Rukh Khan, performances across the board fall flat, failing to evoke any genuine emotion or connection with the audience. Khan's presence, rather than elevating the film, seems wasted amidst a sea of uninspired performances.

Furthermore, the dialogue in "Jawan" is painfully contrived and devoid of any real depth or impact. Viewers are subjected to a barrage of clichés and uninspired lines that do little to enhance the viewing experience. Instead of adding substance to the characters or the narrative, the dialogue serves only to highlight the film's overall lack of creativity.

Visually, "Jawan" fails to impress, resembling a low-budget imitation of more successful films like "Shivaji The Boss." The production values are subpar, and the direction lacks finesse, resulting in a disjointed and unpolished final product.

In conclusion, "Jawan" is a forgettable film that offers little in the way of entertainment or originality. With its recycled plot, lackluster performances, and uninspired dialogue, it fails to leave a lasting impression on viewers. Unless you're a die-hard fan of the genre or Shah Rukh Khan, "Jawan" is best avoided as there are far better cinematic offerings available.

Title: The story is engaging and full of twists and turns
review:

The cinematography is top-notch, and the special effects are impressive.

The film is directed by Atlee, who is known for his masala films with a social message. Jawan is no exception, as it tackles themes of corruption and injustice. However, the film never feels preachy, and instead delivers its message through a fast-paced and entertaining story.

Title: Save yourself few hours and bucks
review:
Shahrukh's shenanigans won't work their charm with a baseless foundation, that is Jawan.

With the jubilant rush and high adrenaline, I went to the theatre for Jawan, met with a severe and jaw numbing reaction ~ is this what fans are cheering for? Critics are going gaga or all that is a subtle hint at jeering.

One critic ~ at least one, should have showed the guts to call out what it is ~ a disaster of epic proportions.

I wrote a reaction here on IMDB but not sure if I posted correctly or not, so here I am rewriting it. It is Sunday and I still haven't come out of the mental setback.

Let us break it down - why it doesn't work.

1. There are such things as continuity and theatrics. They go hand in hand and not one against the other. When people and forces fly from one corner to another of Mumbai in a matter of seconds, I realised it was a movie to keep logic in your back pocket but as the movie progressed, I realised I was wrong, it lowered the IQ of the entire theatre.

2. Such promising young actors and such veterans ~ they all failed to leave a mark because the demand of the story was not convincing enough. Not one performance strikes the chords and like I said, Shahrukh's shenanigans ~ they have run its due course. A stalwart like Vijay Setupati ~ don't know why he was made to look more adorable than scary. Deepika was bright in a short presence but then her character didn't justify it. The inconsistencies were far too blatant to be overlooked. Whenever Sharukh plays the older character, the motivation and actions of the character doesn't chime right - he remains confused most of the times, only to deliver one comeback punch, which seems an extremely poor screenplay founded by even poorer writing.

3. Too many things are packed into the film ~ from drama, emotions, dance, patriotism, laughter and what not but they fail to latch onto a central theme, because it is so weak. I agree it is designed for mass but it confuses the viewer.

4. Music - well let us rather have a two minute silence for that.

Lastly, I admire some of the genuine and realistic works some of the directors from South are coming up with and I love the screen presence of some pan India films have been achieveing lately. But - Jawan - in a right mind, is a disaster of epic proportions.

Thank you for wasting three precious hours of my life.
