Title: Perfect Mass Movie
review:
Jawan is phenomenally entertaining and consistently engaging. It's Stylish, slick and action-packed film which l has actions, thrills, patriotism and gripping storyline. Shahrukh Khan has once again bounced back after the super success of Pathan with a bang and has made his fans delighted.

The movie begins with a bang introducing Shahrukh Khan as a mysterious man in a remote northeast village. JiVikram Rathore (Shah Rukh Khan), a dynamic soldier who chooses an unconventional way to help the society. He hijacks a metro train and holds the passengers as the hostages & puts forth his demands to Khaali Gaikwad (Vijay Sethupathi) Rest of the movie is all about Shahrukh Khan & Shahrukh Khan fighting against crony capitalism, bad loans, farmer suicides, neglected government hospitals, defective military weapons & dirty politics. This is what the film is about.

Jawan is a hard-hitting, exhilarating and equally thought-provoking action thriller where director Atlee uses the protagonist to convey a number of messages and says things that need to be said.

Talking of performances, SRK deliver exceptional performances. He looks very dashing and his performance is outstanding. He is brilliant in every frame. In some of the emotional moments, he's quite endearing. Without any doubt, SRK is the most charming guy in Bollywood. He's stylish, he's confident and has a different aura around him in this film. He truly leaves a mark in both his roles as Vikram Rathore and Azad.

Vijay Sethupathi brings his charm and gravitas. He is powerful and menacing. Nayanthara looks breathtaking in each and every frame she's in. She carries her role with Elan & has performed stunts effortlessly.

Deepika Padukone in a memorable special appearance has created an impact in her role. Priyamani, Sanya Malhotra, Ridhi Dogra, Lehar Khan, and Girija Oak are adequate in their respective roles.

Director Atlee is known for packing his movies with good entertainment elements, and with Jawan, too, he does the same. He presented Shah Rukh in the way fans wish to see him. Story is loaded with twists and turns. Screenplay is effective and engaging for most parts.

Anirudh's background is as-usually good, and it helps the film to go a few notches higher. GK Vishnu's cinematography deserves a special mention The film abounds in special effects and some of them are at par with international standards

Overall, Jawan is a action entertainer which has style as well as enough twists and turns to keep viewers engrossed.

Title: Did this movie really earn this much money???? 😳😳😳
review: Well what can I say. This movie is a dud and so is SRKs acting. Over the top action, over acting. Why can't india make realistic action movies? Why it has to be super human impossible action? The editing could have been better. There was no flow in the story. Some action sequences were decent but apart from that the entire movie felt dull, pointless and just impossible to watch. Songs were ok. The background whistle was super irritating. If this movie would have been released as a parody would have actually worked for them. I saw marvel, mission impossible and 80s action sequences through out this movie.

Title: Mix Veg but at it best
review:
SRK's Dad character which is also played by SRK is iconic.

If you seen Kamal Hassan's Vikram then you will find many similarities. I would still prefer Vikram over Jawan. Undoubtedly SRK has given his 100% but in the end I felt bit boring cos the message given in this movie was taken too far in the last few minutes.

Still worth watching, fully family movie, My Friend created hype and I got influenced for the movie jawan but still Theatre experience will blow your mind and seriously Vikram Rathore will not disappoint you. Trust me.

Go for it and personal suggestion to watch Kamal Hassan's Vikram.

Title: Shahrukh Khan's "Jawan" Delivers an Action-packed Masterpiece
MASS with good claas and powerpacked with social and political issues.
review:
SRK' s coolest movie ever exception DON series , movie has been delivered with a social message and attack on system failure through sideby stories and a political advise speech has been delivered to audiences too. Looking at recent environment and event its relatable to our society and political scenario in country.

But these stories and messages take backseat when main story of the movie takes place .

A good script movie with unbelievable screen presence of SRK he is backbone of the film specially Vikram rathod role. You will just crave for vikram rathod presence .

First half could have been better although it'll never get bored even for mature audience too.

But second half is just excellent confrontation of vikram rathod and kali keeps you engaged in movie tightly and special mention to anirudh music that is decent BGM can't get better than this .

Dialogue is finely written by sumit arora not at his best but good enough .specially a scene where vijay sethupati (kali) says "kya scene hai" meme material of modi g. IYKYK
IYKYK coulb be perfect title for first half because it is directly relatable to recent events happened in our country . Movie keep bashing system .

Overall It is a decent powerpacked entertainer, you surely should watch this movie this weekend.

Title: Not bad but also not that great.
review: One mistake in the review cring not sometimes but many a times in this movie. The movie had a good story line by the director failed to portray it correctly and few things like overacting of srk in few scenes, unwanted comedy, cring action scenes which make science and reality feel like rip, also songs aired at incorrect timings made a mocery of the entire film. One must understand that if a film contains social message then songs must be tuned as such so that people can relate with it. If these things could have been avoided then this film could have been great film but unfortunately the film is only running by the hype created bh the pr team. More like a semi hit not not a blockbuster at all. I hope that dhunki will be better that this jawan and previous pathan.

Title: Amazing Action Thriller
review:
Nothing like Indian Cinema has witnessed before.

Every part of the movie is intense and interesting, Eyes always glued to the screen waiting for wgatp happens next.

The twists and suspense and then the aatisfaction of justice makes the whole experience feel great
Cinemas are still packed and epveryone who came out of the theatre after the movie was amazed by how good the movie was.

Audience reaction intense during scenes of the movie.

Dialogues were great and to the point.

No bollywood movie with the same concept ever before in indian cinema.

Do not miss the best experience of Indian Cinema.

Title: Story seriously lacks a spine but an entertainer nevertheless
Mind-blowing!! Explosive!!
review: What a movie by Atlee & SRK!!! Loved every bit of it! Beautiful story! Phenomenal screen presence & aura of the King Khan! He is a charmer! Nayanthara looked sublime!! Vijay Setupathi & Deepika were Ok. It was a treat to watch! They only drawback is the immemorable songs but the bgm was impeccable! When a Bollywood movie is made so perfect & still the music is not upto the point, it pinches! Nevertheless, proud of SRK, Atlee & the whole team of Jawan for churning out this BLOCKBUSTER!! Thoroughly enjoyable cinema! A must watch on the big screen! This year's 2nd best action film after Mission Impossible - 5!!

Title: Must Watch Movie
review:
The movie was very beautiful and its story was good enough. You won't be able to look away from the screen. Everyone's acting was really good, there is no controversial or adult scene in this movie, it will hold your attention like glue on the screen. There's a super shocking twist in the middle that I didn't expect, and if I tell you that, it might spoil your enjoyment of the movie. In the last two or three years, this is a movie where you get to enjoy the scene as much as you want in front of the screen. Thank you Atlii and SRK sir. This is a movie to watch with family and friends. Really enjoy it. Must watch movie.

My rating will be 9.0 + as a SRK fan 10/10.

Title: Cinematic Gem By Mixing Stories In A Classy Manner
review: Massiest Movie Ever Being Witnessed By Bollywood By A South Director. The Main Credit Goes To My College Senior From Sathyabama University Atlee Who Has Created This Magic Like His Other Movies But This Time It Is Next Level. No Words To Describe The Style ,Swag Of Shahrukh Khan. Those Who Have Already Seen Earlier Movies Of Atlee Like Mershal,Bigil,Theri,Raja Rani They Can Predict The Storyline As I Personally Feel That The Story Was A Cocktail Of All His Previous South Movies. But Everything Is Justified When It Is Being Portrayed In The Screen In Such A Classy Manner And Acted By Shahrukh Khan. Ignore The Songs. A One Time Must Watch Movie.

Title: Amazing movie
review: One of the best movie from sharukh khan. Show the reality of Indian politics. I have never seen such an amazing movie. Sharukh has done a great job. Also there are other talented actors who give their best. Will recommend to everyone. Today Indian politics is at worst place. Everyone should watch this movie and think what they are going to support in terms of giving vote to that politicians. This movie show the exact face of the politicians who just don't care once they won after the elections. Movie has such emotions and the truth that sharukh and team want to spread is worth the everything. Love you sharukh.

Title: Absolute Masterpiece!!!!
review: "Jawan" is a gripping Hindi action film that packs a punch. Sai Dharam Tej delivers a standout performance as Jai, a soldier torn between duty and family. The action sequences are heart-pounding, choreographed with precision. The supporting cast, including Mehreen Pirzada and Prasanna, adds depth to the narrative. Thaman S's music and background score enhance the emotional impact. Director BVS Ravi's storytelling skillfully balances action and drama, paying tribute to the sacrifices of soldiers. "Jawan" is a must-watch for its compelling storyline, strong performances, and patriotic message. All in all it was one hell of a movie!

Title: Boring
review:
"Jawaan" offers a familiar narrative within the Bollywood action genre, showcasing a dedicated soldier facing internal conflicts while battling external threats. While the film boasts commendable performances from its cast, particularly the lead actor who effectively conveys the emotional turmoil of his character, the plot falls prey to clichés and lacks originality.

The screenplay seems borrowed from previous successful Bollywood military dramas, resulting in a sense of déjà vu for seasoned moviegoers. The action sequences, though well-executed, fail to bring a fresh perspective to the genre, making them predictable and repetitive.

Furthermore, the film struggles with pacing issues, making certain segments feel prolonged and tedious. The dialogues, while attempting to inject emotion, often come across as melodramatic and formulaic, contributing to a sense of predictability.

In essence, "Jawaan" may cater to audiences seeking routine action-packed entertainment, but its lack of innovation and reliance on tried-and-tested formulas may leave discerning viewers yearning for a more refreshing cinematic experience. It's a watchable film for those who enjoy conventional Bollywood action, but it falls short in offering something distinctive to the genre.

Title: One of best srk movie but some flaws
review: Biggest flaw is Vijay sathepathi he dosent even match up to the character of srk. I mean come one that guy can't speak straight Hindi he should have learned it from super heroes of South India like rajnikant . Speaking in accent is stupid and his monotonous acting is just the only reason I gave it 8 and not 10 as of srk he has just nailed it every aspect of the character. The director of course ain't perfect too many random songs too frequent. So basically the story which of course is written by the director himself , if story and srk would have been given to someone and minus Vijay the film would have been great. But anyways srk nails it.

Title: Way way way better than pathan
review: Not a usual one to review but this movie has been awesome to watch for me right from the beginning.. till the end, I would not deny. I agree this is a movie of shah rukh khan and vijay Sethu pathi. However you can't deny the fact that each and every actor have given their heart to their role.. so everybody look awesome. Songs may not be catchy except for a one or two which the film maker should take lessons for the next one. However, a good watch surpasses even peppy numbers n this movie is the best example for that .. best wishes to each and every face of this movie. I loved watching this at Edinburgh, Odeon.

Title: Mass done with Class
review: Atlee has delivered what Bollywood couldn't deliver over the years. Presented SRK in a never seen avatar. Action, Emotion, Romance you will get everything. Complete waste of time.

Action is worse than in Pathaan & SRK's overacting is painful to watch. Yes, there is novelty in makeup but that only adds to the over-the-top feel.

There is nothing redeeming in the movie - songs are forgettable, dance is unattractive. I think the producer/ director decided that they don't need much of a story if SRK is the lead but SRK's performance is perhaps the biggest negative about the movie!

Title: Rip all record Blockbuster king khan
Jawan: A Visually Stunning Action Thriller
review:
Jawan is a 2023 Indian Tamil-language action thriller film written and directed by Atlee and produced by Sun Pictures. The film stars Shah Rukh Khan, Nayanthara, Sanya Malhotra, and Vijay Sethupathi. The film follows a mysterious man on a mission to avenge the death of his loved ones.

Jawan is a well-made commercial entertainer that has all the elements to please the audience and Shah Rukh Khan fans. There are pulse-pounding moments, high-octane action scenes, electrifying soundtracks, and some stunning visuals. Khan is in top form, and he delivers a double role with ease. He is both charming and menacing, and he brings a lot of charisma to the role. Nayanthara is also excellent, and she has great chemistry with Khan. Sanya Malhotra and Vijay Sethupathi are also good in their supporting roles.

The action sequences in Jawan are some of the best that have been seen in a Tamil film in recent years. They are well-choreographed and exciting, and they keep the audience on the edge of their seats. The film also has a good social message about the importance of fighting against injustice.

Here are some of the things that make Jawan a good film:

1. The action sequences are well-choreographed and exciting.

2. Shah Rukh Khan gives a double role performance with ease.

3. Nayanthara and Sanya Malhotra are also excellent in their supporting roles.

4. The film has a good social message about the importance of fighting against injustice.

Here are some of the things that could have been improved:
1. The film is a bit slow in the second half.

2. The social message could have been conveyed in an even better manner.

Overall, Jawan is a well-made and entertaining film that is sure to please fans of action thrillers. It is a must-watch for all Shah Rukh Khan fans.

Title: A fantastic storyline butchered by cliches and SRK playing Rajnikant - but it was fun
review:
Of course the avengers were fun..
So why not park your brain at home and forget physics laws and just enjoy a superhero movie Bollywood style

Except , the cliches, the schoolboy errors where the dubbing doesn't match the visuals, item numbers jarring entries( for example after a poignant moment about farmer suicides), stupefying old school Indian action all spoil what was a very good premise.

SRK gives you what a fan of his would want- some moments of good intensity followed by standard expressions

Vijay sethupathy us clearly out of place and comes across as half baked in the role he is supposed to play

Nayanthara does a decent job and deepika has a token role but makes an impact

However my biggest disappointment came when I heard the background score and music.. Except for the excellent song Challeya ( performed beautifully by Arijit and Shilpa Rao) it is just a lot of speaking in tongues and music that any generative AI could ve generated...

Watch the movie if you like mindless fun( which is fun) and for the hilarious cliches - like deepika fainting at the gallows or the stuff that the current generation laughs at when we watch old formula driven South Indian movies.

This movie will give u everything emotion, action, comedy, storyline, thrill;the direction is perfect mind blowing action sequences best camera angles awesome vfx nd colourgrading. It is a rollercoaster ride, full package of mass level action and thrill, starcast is perfect everyone has done very good work and not to forget the bgm given by anirudh which will make this experience even better. The presentation of srk is awesome.overall i will say it is a movie not to be missed in theatres so go watch it without hesitation u will not be disappointed and last but not the least the so many different looks of srk 🔥🔥🔥

Title: Cinematic Brilliance Meets Familiar Tropes: A Review of "Jawan"
review:
"Jawan" is a film that immediately grabs your attention with its impressive cinematography. The opening sequences are visually striking, setting a high bar for the rest of the movie. The introduction of the hero is well-executed, drawing viewers into the story right from the start. However, as the film progresses, it becomes evident that while it has its merits, it also suffers from some common Bollywood tropes.

One of the main drawbacks of "Jawan" is its somewhat predictable storyline. The plot follows a well-trodden path, resembling the typical formulaic narratives that are often seen in Bollywood films. While this familiarity might resonate with some audiences, it does leave others longing for more originality and creativity.

A notable aspect of the film is Shah Rukh Khan's double role as a father and son. While Khan is a talented actor, this dual portrayal feels somewhat overused, reminiscent of his previous roles in movies like "Ra. One" and "Badshah." Additionally, the film's plot bears striking similarities to the popular series "Money Heist," which may lead some viewers to feel that "Jawan" lacks a unique identity of its own.

On a positive note, the cameo appearance of Sanjay Dutt adds a touch of star power to the film. Dutt's presence is always a welcome addition to any Bollywood production, and his brief appearance in "Jawan" is no exception.

Turning our attention to the music in the film, it is worth noting that the songs may not be as emotionally connected to the storyline as one might hope. Music often plays a pivotal role in Bollywood movies, enhancing the audience's emotional engagement with the characters and the plot. In this regard, "Jawan" falls short, failing to establish a strong emotional connection through its music.

Despite these shortcomings in the story and music department, "Jawan" shines when it comes to its cinematography. The visuals are consistently impressive throughout the film, showcasing the talent of the cinematographer and the director in capturing breathtaking moments on screen. The level of detail and the artistic choices made in framing shots contribute significantly to the overall visual experience of the movie.

Moreover, the film's audio and sound design are on par with its cinematography, creating an immersive and engaging auditory experience. The sound effects and background score complement the visuals, enhancing the overall impact of key scenes.

One notable flaw in the film is the underdeveloped portrayal of the villain. Villains play a crucial role in adding depth and conflict to a story, and in "Jawan," the antagonist is not given enough screen time or character development to truly shine. This missed opportunity to create a compelling villain hampers the film's ability to build tension and excitement.

In conclusion, "Jawan" is a movie with a mixed bag of strengths and weaknesses. While it excels in terms of cinematography and audio-visual experience, it falls short in terms of its somewhat clichéd storyline, reliance on familiar Bollywood tropes, and underdeveloped antagonist. The presence of a seasoned actor like Shah Rukh Khan and a memorable cameo by Sanjay Dutt adds to its appeal. Ultimately, "Jawan" is a visually and aurally pleasing film that could have benefited from a more original and well-developed narrative.

Title: A Familiar Journey with Modern Flair
review:
"Jawan" takes audiences on a nostalgic trip through Bollywood's golden era, presenting a story that feels all too familiar. While the film attempts to infuse a breath of fresh air with modern filmography, it struggles to break free from the shadows of its predecessors.

The plot is a well-trodden path, reminiscent of classic Bollywood films, which may evoke a sense of déjà vu for seasoned moviegoers. The narrative lacks the innovation needed to make it stand out in the contemporary cinematic landscape. Despite the film's efforts to incorporate modern storytelling techniques, the core premise feels like a retread of past successes.

However, where "Jawan" excels is in its cinematography and production design. The film showcases a visual spectacle, thanks to the advancements in technology and a keen eye for aesthetics. The action sequences are well-choreographed, providing an adrenaline rush that complements the otherwise predictable storyline.

The performances of the cast deserve praise, with the lead actors delivering earnest portrayals of their characters. Despite the recycled plot, they manage to infuse moments of genuine emotion into their roles, creating a connection with the audience.

The music, though not groundbreaking, serves its purpose, adding an emotional layer to key scenes. However, one can't help but wish for a soundtrack that breaks away from the conventions of yesteryear.

In summary, "Jawan" is a mixed bag. It successfully blends modern film techniques with a story that feels like a tribute to Bollywood's cinematic history. While it may not offer a groundbreaking narrative, the film manages to entertain with its visual flair and committed performances. If you're a fan of classic Bollywood storytelling with a contemporary twist, "Jawan" might just hit the right notes for you. However, for those seeking originality and a fresh take on the genre, the film falls short of delivering a truly memorable experience.

Title: Jawan for north indians, buddhah for south indians
review:
Azad(srk) is a jailer of woman's cell. He gathers a group of women prisoners and does several vigilante works mainly targeting weapons dealer kaalee(vijay sethupathi) and names himself vikram rathore. Special officer Narmada (nayanthara) is assigned to catch azad. Meanwhile azad's marriage is fixed with narmada itself who is a single mother.

On their wedding night, kaalee's brother attack and kidnap azad and narmada. While they are being tortured, the real vikram rathore (srk) saves azad and kill kaalee's brother. Who is vikram rathore and how is azad related to him is the rest of the movie.

Before watching the movie itself, i kept in my mind that the film would be extremely predictable and a mixture of various films. Atlee this time instead of copying other films copied his own films. But the problem is unlike atlee's previous films, jawan fails to pack an emotional punch. Only the aarariraro song evokes some emotions. Vijay sethupathi is wasted as the villain. Unlike mahendran in theri and sj suryah in mersal, vijay sethupathi's performance fails to evoke any hatred towards him. First half provided some decent entertainment but second half fails to emotionally connect us and gets very lengthy. Sanjay dutt's cameo doesn't make much impact.

On the whole, watching jawan gives you a feeling of a vijay film. But the hero is srk. Looks like atlee wanted to make this film with vijay, but since vijay was not available he went to srk. Atlee thought that north indians would not have seen his films so he mixed all his previous films into this one.

Title: Some illogical stuff indeed

Every character was uninteresting without being introduced properly.

SRK works as a jailer in a woman prison and gets along so well with the female inmates so well that one of the female inmates starts singing with a guy's voice and then dance to the Zinda Banda song and they took the time to light up the prison as a disco.

Nayanthara's character fell in love with SRK and married him after just one song Challya and didn't consider the possiblity that the marriage could of been an ambush.

The old SRK doesn't remember anything but still saves his son before the interval.

The action was atrocious with old men on scooters taking out big lorries. Srk threw a cigar in the motorcycle fuel tank but conveniently didn't explode until it hit the other vehicles and expected the audience to except it.

SRK had a team of 6 girls and not a single one was likeable.

So the amount of stupidity certainly did cross the limits.

review:

Title: A Thunderous Return of the King - Jawan is a Mass Entertainer with a Message
A stale Reel
review:
Awan," a recent addition to the world of cinema, had many viewers like myself hoping for a fresh and exciting experience. However, it turned out to be a lackluster film that left us yearning for more.

One of the most glaring issues with "Jawan" is its lack of originality. It felt like a recycled version of movies we've seen in the Tamil industry before, offering nothing novel or refreshing. The absence of a unique storyline left a void that no amount of flashy visuals or charismatic performances could fill.

The film also suffers from a sense of déjà vu, with scenes seemingly copied from other movies, making it difficult to immerse oneself in the narrative. It's as if the filmmakers stitched together a patchwork of familiar moments, resulting in a disjointed and unengaging plot.

The characters in "Jawan" failed to leave a lasting impact. They lacked depth and development, making it challenging to invest emotionally in their journeys. The protagonist's struggle felt contrived, and his motivations remained unclear, leaving the audience indifferent to his plight.

While there were attempts to infuse the film with high-octane action sequences and impressive cinematography, these elements couldn't salvage the overall viewing experience. It was as if the filmmakers were attempting to distract us from the lack of substance with superficial gloss.

In conclusion, "Jawan" is a disappointment for those seeking innovation and originality in cinema. It's a lackluster movie that feels like a mere rehash of what we've seen in the Tamil industry previously. With a weak plot, borrowed scenes, and underdeveloped characters, "Jawan" fails to leave a lasting impression and leaves its viewers yearning for something more engaging and fresh.

Title: JAWAN is a MASS-BLOCKBUSTER
review:
JAWAN is a MASS-BLOCKBUSTER that will have you laughing, sobbing, and cheering. It's a monument to Shah Rukh Khan's enduring legacy and a reminder of why we all fell in love with Bollywood in the first place. Don't pass up the opportunity to see this cinematic masterpiece, which will certainly go down as one of the best Indian films of all time. Shah Rukh Khan, you've done it again, and we can't wait to see what you've got in store for us next.

The supporting cast of JAWAN deserves special mention, with each actor delivering a stellar performance. Their chemistry with Shah Rukh Khan is palpable, adding depth and authenticity to the story. The film's antagonist, played by a formidable actor, is menacing and compelling, creating an intense conflict that keeps you on the edge of your seat.

Title: Wafer thin plot extended to near 3 hours in the extended cut
review:
Jawan, the latest addition to the action genre, falls short of expectations with a plot that brings nothing new to the table. The storyline follows the clichéd trajectory of a patriotic soldier with a vendetta against the corrupt system, a narrative that has been explored ad nauseam in countless other films.

One of the major drawbacks of Jawan is its reliance on slow-motion action scenes, a tired and overused technique that fails to inject any excitement into the movie. Instead of enhancing the viewing experience, these sequences come off as tedious and uninspired, leaving the audience craving for something more dynamic and engaging.

The weak link in Jawan is undoubtedly the villain character, which lacks the depth and complexity needed to create a formidable antagonist. The antagonist's motivations are unclear, and the performance lacks the intensity required to make a lasting impact. This weak portrayal diminishes the overall stakes in the film and leaves the audience indifferent to the conflict at hand.

Despite a decent performance from the lead actor, the lack of innovation in the plot, coupled with ineffective slow-motion action scenes and a feeble antagonist, makes Jawan a forgettable addition to the action genre. For viewers seeking a fresh and gripping cinematic experience, Jawan might not be the best choice.

Title: A Mixed Bag with Uneven Performances
review:
Jawan presents itself as a film with a commendable storyline that unfortunately falls short due to its lackluster editing. While it seemingly aims to cater to the South Indian cinema audience, the inclusion of Shah Rukh Khan seems to demand a dash of Bollywood flair, an expectation that sadly remains unmet.

The movie seems to diverge into a series of unnecessary songs that unfortunately feel more like a detour than a complement to the plot. These musical interludes appear forced, detracting from the primary narrative and consuming valuable screen time that could have been utilized to enhance the core storyline.

That said, the acting prowess of the cast, particularly Vijay Sethupathi, stands out, offering moments of brilliance and depth. The performances effectively portray a range of emotions, showcasing the potential that lies within the script. However, Shah Rukh Khan's portrayal feels somewhat self-centric, overshadowing the ensemble's collective performance and steering the narrative towards a more mono-dimensional focus.

Director Atlee certainly embarks on a journey to craft a hit with Jawan but is undercut by a performance that seems to prioritize individual limelight over a cohesive storytelling experience. This emphasis on a central character dampens the collaborative spirit typically seen in ensemble casts, leaving audiences with a feeling of what could have been.

In conclusion, while Jawan offers a solid story and showcases noteworthy performances from most of its cast, the final product is hampered by uneven editing and a not-so-impressive turn from Shah Rukh Khan. It stands as a movie with moments of promise, yet ultimately succumbs to a series of missteps that prevent it from reaching great heights. It is, regrettably, a missed opportunity for what could have been a harmonious blend of South Indian cinema and Bollywood magic.

Title: Just watch with no brains
review:
Shahrukh Khan is not merely a name; he is a brand that needs no introduction. After dabbling in non-commercial movies and facing the usual criticisms, the actor, deemed too old for romance, returns to the screen for the second time in a year with a completely senseless yet quintessentially Hindi cinema super action movie.

In the realm of Indian cinema, one doesn't have to delve into the supernatural; rather, the lead character conquers the seemingly impossible. The movie follows this formula, with a vigilante attempting to resolve the country's issues in the blink of an eye-a concept that has proven successful in the south but is yet to be fully embraced in Hindi cinema.

Several factors contributed to the movie's success:

SRK had already delivered a blockbuster, setting high expectations for this film.

Atlee Kumar's presentation is tailor-made for the big screen. Despite lacking logical coherence, the grandiosity of his style resonates well with the audience. Large-scale sequences and the adept portrayal of multiple characters in a single frame are Atlee's forte, once again proving successful.

Nayanthara's inclusion was surprising, but after watching the movie, it becomes clear why she was cast-her commendable skills added value to the film.

So, if you're in the mood for a purely entertaining, "Paisa Wasool" (Money Recovered) experience, with a complete disregard for sense and rationality, and you're seeking continuous action with zero expectations, then this movie is for you.

On a side note, SRK continues to be the King, showcasing his skills despite the movie's absurdities.

Title: Good, but not the best...
review:
Just watched Jawan. Here is my honest review -
1) Very good content and social messaging, but the delivery was not up to the mark.

2) Too many songs in the movie, extremely unnecessary, which increased the movie runtime.

3) The editor did a very poor job in managing the scenes. So many songs, no balance between the pre and the post scenes.

4) Cinematography is awesome, but the screenplay was below average. Story telling could have been better.

5) Atlee Kumar, who directed popular movies like Theri, Raja Rani, Mersal, etc. Has not done his job properly for this movie. He could have done much better because I know this director has potential.

6) The ending is very poor. Having portrayed a villain like Vijay Sethupathi, he should have been more dangerous and aggressive, rather than being comedic.

7) SRK, Nayanthara and the other ladies did great, no doubt.

8) WTF Sanjay Dutt was doing there. He was brought in for a very crucial role, but he made a super joke of himself by making a serious scene comedy.

9) Music, BGM was good. But at some places, it could have been subtle.

10) The hype caused due to the trailer; the movie is nowhere near it.

11) We have witnessed mass movies like KGF & RRR, building public expectations so high. While releasing a mass movie like these, the team should keep this is serious consideration and make movies up to that level. Stardom is not always everything. If this continues, then in no time people will stop going to theatres and start hating SRK like they hate Salman Khan.

12) A film this big, super-hyped, must leave a strong impact, not just with the content and dialogues, but with the other aspects of moviemaking.

13) Some one who is honest would say Pathaan was better made than Jawan.

14) At last, this is a one-time movie to watch. You all can watch it and share your own thoughts. Surprisingly, the theatres are not fully occupied, you can buy tickets easily.

Title: Just excellent 👌 movie, Great story
review:
I am not someone who watches many movies. I have to be really convinced to spend my time on one. But based on my experience, I must say that Jawan is a film you should watch at least once in your life. If you are thinking about it, just go for it. It is an experience that is both thrilling and meaningful.

Jawan is a true Bollywood masala film, which means it has everything packed into one movie. It has high-energy action, emotional drama, catchy songs, and a powerful message. The star of the show is Shah Rukh Khan, and he delivers a performance that is simply incredible. In fact, he plays not one, but two main roles-a father and a son. Both characters are strong, passionate, and fighting for justice in their own ways.

The story revolves around a man named Azad, who leads a team of fierce women on missions to fight corruption and help the common people. Their goal is to force the system to fix its flaws. The film does an excellent job of showing real problems that affect India today, like farmer struggles, faulty healthcare, and political greed. It makes you cheer for the heroes because their fight feels so real and important.

The action sequences are a major highlight. They are big, loud, and brilliantly shot. You will see thrilling fights on a train, in a prison, and in many other unexpected places. The director, Atlee, has filled the movie with style and moments that will make you want to clap and cheer. The music is also very good, with songs like "Chaleya" that are sweet and "Zinda Banda" that is a pure dance anthem.

But Jawan is not just about action and style. At its heart, it is an emotional story about a son's promise to his father and a fight for a better country. It will make you think, and it might even bring a tear to your eye.

In short, Jawan is more than just a movie; it is an event. It is a powerful, entertaining, and satisfying film that reminds you of the power of cinema. It is a complete package that deserves to be seen on the big screen. So, take my advice and just go for it. You will not be disappointed.

Title: A Decent Attempt That Loses Its Way
review:
Jawan starts off well with a strong story and an interesting plot, but somewhere down the line, the film loses focus. The father-son angle, though emotional, diverts the movie from its core theme and makes it feel stretched and confused.

The direction is just about okay, nothing remarkable, and the acting - while fine - is definitely below expectations, especially from such a talented ensemble cast. It's disappointing to see that Shah Rukh Khan seems more focused on showcasing his physique than delivering the kind of powerful performances he's known for.

Despite its scale and potential, Jawan doesn't quite live up to the hype. It's a decent one-time watch but far from SRK's best.

Title: Falls short of expectations.
review:
Jawan had all the makings of a blockbuster with its high budget, star-studded cast, and promise of action-packed drama, but ultimately falls short of expectations. The premise, revolving around a vigilante on a mission to challenge the corrupt elite, feels tired and overly familiar-reminiscent of past films like Vikram and Hindustani. It's disappointing that with such a big budget, Bollywood chose to tread a path we've already seen many times before, highlighting a lack of creativity at the script level. The hype around the film feels unearned, as the story fails to bring anything new to the table.

The film does start off strong, with an exciting, well-choreographed fight scene that promises a thrilling ride ahead. Shah Rukh Khan's screen presence is electrifying, and he truly shines in the action-packed sequences. The fight scene just before the interval is another highlight, shot with high energy and intensity, and in typical Rohit Shetty and South Indian style with a generous dose of action and drama. Khan's commitment to his role is palpable-he gives it his all, making his performance one of the few redeeming aspects of the film. The background music, while decent, occasionally becomes overwhelming, but the songs Banda Zinda and Chaleya are catchy and stick with you.

However, as the film progresses, the weaknesses become more apparent. The script falters, shifting from unrealistic to outright absurd as it nears the climax. The inclusion of too many flashbacks, sub-plots, and songs only drags down the pace, making the already long runtime of 170 minutes feel even more sluggish. The female cast, featuring talented actors like Sania Malhotra and Priya Mani, are sorely underused, their characters sidelined in favor of an SRK-centric narrative. Vijay Sethupathi, an actor known for his stellar performances, is wasted in a role that fails to do justice to his talent, thanks to the weak script. Even Sanjay Dutt's cameo at the end feels out of place and unnecessary-likely more a gesture of friendship with SRK than any real artistic choice.

Overall, Jawan is a mediocre film that has been blown out of proportion by both the makers and critics. Despite its potential and some good action sequences, the film is bogged down by an uninspired script and underdeveloped characters. If you were hoping for another *Pathaan*-level experience, this isn't it. It's an average film at best, and frankly, a bit of a letdown.

A wake up call to every Indian - a powerful message from the Khan himself. Sure one should appreciate Atlee's audacity to challenge the Indian government.

Story:
Vikram Rathore (Sharukh Khan) is a vigilante with intentions of doing good to people. Narmada Rai (Nayanthara) is the officer in charge to capture Vikram. Kalee Gaikwad (Vijay Sethupathi) is the symbolification of all evil and is the only thing that would bring closure to this typical tale.

Jawan runs fast and its characters run faster. Timelines skip. Tracks mingle. Logic dies as time passess but it is kind of interesting to watch initially. Predictablity kills curiosity later. Audience say 'ugh'. Vikram doesn't stop. Flashback sequences. Bullets firing in the air. Cringe moments.

But there is a quality in production that is admirable. Anirudh's music saves Jawan. Fresh air of breath to the Hindi cinema for sure. All in all Jawan is mediocre. You could watch it after its release. Better to watch in Hindi version with subtitles. Tamil version has bonus scenes though.

Title: Absolutely rubbish

But no, the director resorts to copying small parts from a million Hollywood movies including The Matrix, Bourne Identity, and Mission Impossible.

The recent wave of South Indian movies gaining popularity across India means Bollywood actors are also resorting to the same formula of larger than life characters. However this doesn't mean that Bollywood takes its audience for a ride with poor storytelling and beyond a point, some folks will stop watching these movies in theatres.

Title: Sagar Jarvis
review:
Hatts off to NAYANTHARA, she nailed it. She single handedly runs the first half.

In the first half of the movie, the weightage of NAYANTHARA & SRK is seen to be the same. But in the second half the role of NAYANTHARA has been reduced to make SRK appear bigger. Or it just feels that she's equal to none.

Had her weightage been maintained in the second half, then perhaps the film would have ended on a different note. But in Bollywood, often at the end of the movie, the hero has to do a big portrayal, perhaps Nayanthara suffered because of this.

And also if we talk about VIJAY SETHUPATI, he has not able to give full justice to his talent in all the bollywood films or webseries he has worked in till now. His acting seems same in every project. And he played the role of 'Kali Gaikwad' in this movie, which is a maharashtrian character.

He is doing a Hindi film, his character is Marathi and the tunning of Hindi language seems like he's delivering dialogues in Tamil. Feels like director has played a dirty joke with the VIJAY SETHUPATI & also with the audience by casting him.

DEEPIKA's cameo was a very bad experience, after her entry the pace of the movie slows down.

The movie starts getting boring only after her entry. The excitement which was maintained by srk and nayanthara before the interval, DEEPIKA completely ruins the plot with her arrival.

Overall it deserves not much more than 3.5 stars.

2 stars for all the good stuff.

And 1.5 stars for NAYANTHARA.

Because she single handedly uplifts the first half.

We expected a roller coaster ride from Atlee, but he miserably fails to deliver this time.

Overall disappointed a lot.

Title: Everything A Hindi Film Should Be
review:
I watched this movie twice in the cinema and still counting. It is very enjoyable, filled with drama and action, and simply entertaining and heartwarming.

The direction and cinematography of this film are outstanding; nonetheless, the musical score is on point. Dance numbers are extravagant and movement-enticing; moreover, it is a story that is emotional, brave, and thrilling.

Watching this movie can be a bit overwhelming, since the scenes are heavily edited, and the pace is relatively fast. That could be considered a disadvantage, but at the same time, it is actually what makes this movie satisfying to watch. The acting is average in some scenes, but excellent in others.

It is best to look at this movie as chapters; each narrative is related to the previous one; to visualize and complete the storyline. There are some aspects I would have liked the filmmakers to elaborate more on, but that leaves a room for a sequel.

All in all, I advise you to watch this movie in the theater to experience it as intended.

Title: Review of movie
review:
Jawan is phenomenally entertaining and consistently engaging. It's Stylish, slick and action-packed film which l has actions, thrills, patriotism and gripping storyline. Shahrukh Khan has once again bounced back after the super success of Pathan with a bang and has made his fans delighted.

The movie begins with a bang introducing Shahrukh Khan as a mysterious man in a remote northeast village. JiVikram Rathore (Shah Rukh Khan), a dynamic soldier who chooses an unconventional way to help the society. He hijacks a metro train and holds the passengers as the hostages & puts forth his demands to Khaali Gaikwad (Vijay Sethupathi) Rest of the movie is all about Shahrukh Khan & Shahrukh Khan fighting against crony capitalism, bad loans, farmer suicides, neglected government hospitals, defective military weapons & dirty politics. This is what the film is about.

Jawan is a hard-hitting, exhilarating and equally thought-provoking action thriller where director Atlee uses the protagonist to convey a number of messages and says things that need to be said.

Talking of performances, SRK deliver exceptional performances. He looks very dashing and his performance is outstanding. He is brilliant in every frame. In some of the emotional moments, he's quite endearing. Without any doubt, SRK is the most charming guy in Bollywood. He's stylish, he's confident and has a different aura around him in this film. He truly leaves a mark in both his roles as Vikram Rathore and Azad.

Title: Massieverest
review:
"Jawan" is an exceptional cinematic experience that deserves every bit of its 5-star rating. This film masterfully weaves together a captivating storyline, impeccable direction, and outstanding performances to create a truly memorable viewing experience.

From the very beginning, "Jawan" grabs your attention and never lets go. The narrative is a thrilling rollercoaster of emotions, filled with unexpected twists and turns that keep you on the edge of your seat. It's a perfect blend of action, drama, and suspense.

The direction is nothing short of brilliant. The director's ability to build tension and create intense, nail-biting moments is commendable. The pacing of the film is spot on, ensuring that you're always engaged in the unfolding story.

The cast delivers stellar performances across the board. The lead actor's portrayal of the protagonist is both compelling and convincing. His emotional depth and dedication to the role shine through, making you empathize with his character's journey. The supporting cast members also excel in their respective roles, adding depth and authenticity to the story.

The action sequences in "Jawan" are a true highlight. They are choreographed and executed with precision, leaving you in awe of the sheer intensity and adrenaline-pumping moments. The film's music complements the on-screen drama perfectly, enhancing the overall impact of key scenes.

What sets "Jawan" apart is its ability to tackle relevant social issues within the framework of a gripping action drama. It subtly addresses themes of duty, sacrifice, and patriotism, making it not just an entertaining film but also a thought-provoking one.

In conclusion, "Jawan" is a cinematic gem that should not be missed. It offers a riveting story, exceptional direction, top-notch performances, and thrilling action sequences-all the ingredients for a memorable movie-watching experience. Whether you're a fan of action-packed films or simply appreciate a well-crafted story, "Jawan" delivers on all fronts. It's a 5-star masterpiece that will leave you applauding long after the credits roll.

Title: SRK in Full Force - A Mass Riot with a Message
review:
Jawan is Shah Rukh Khan at his most electric, blending swagger, emotion, and social commentary into a high-voltage action spectacle. Atlee crafts a film that's stylish, fast-paced, and packed with whistle-worthy moments - from the metro hijack intro to the interval twist and the explosive finale. SRK shines in a dual role, effortlessly switching between charm, menace, and patriotism.

The female ensemble - Nayanthara, Deepika Padukone (in a memorable extended cameo), and the all-women squad - adds real weight and freshness to the narrative. Anirudh's soundtrack and background score elevate nearly every scene. While the film occasionally leans into melodrama and over-the-top convenience, the entertainment quotient is so high that these flaws never derail the experience.

Jawan is mass cinema done right: emotional, loud, stylish, and endlessly fun.

Title: Nice movie
review:
Jawan" is a dynamic action film that manages to pack a punch while also delving into profound emotional and social issues. The movie maintains a relentless pace, weaving its narrative with impactful action sequences that are heightened by the presence of genuine emotion.

**Balancing Action and Emotion (8/10):** "Jawan" finds the delicate equilibrium between high-octane action and heartfelt emotion. This unique blend not only entertains but also gives depth to the action sequences, making them more resonant with the audience. It's evident that the filmmakers aimed to create an action film with a heart, and they succeeded in doing so.

**Addressing Social and Political Issues (9/10):** The film takes a bold step by addressing pressing social and political concerns. What's notable is its impartial approach; "Jawan" isn't politically biased but instead scrutinizes the systemic flaws ingrained in our society. This choice ensures that its message remains universally relevant.

**Pacing and Emotional Depth (7/10):** While the movie's fast tempo adds to its excitement, it occasionally hampers the emotional depth of certain scenes. Slowing down at times could have allowed for more profound exploration of the characters' feelings and experiences. Nonetheless, the film manages to forge a connection between the audience and its characters.

**Cinematic Brilliance (9/10):** Visually, "Jawan" is a work of art. The cinematography is nothing short of exceptional, immersing viewers in a visually captivating world. The film's stunning visuals contribute significantly to its overall allure, making it a cinematic treat.

Title: Never Saw housful in Saudi Arabia theaters
review:
I never saw houseful in Saudi Arabia and SRK is our proud which we called by Saudi people as you are from Sharukh county county, hahahaha.

I went in to see a mass movie featuring SRK in an unprecedented, larger-than-life role. Little did I know that I would be in for much more. Atlee has not only met but surpassed all expectations, while RCE VFX studio has set a new benchmark in Indian cinema. Despite its 160-minute runtime, the movie breezed by like a gentle gust of wind.

This movie will took you to old Bollywood where it fearlessly held up a mirror to the establishment and urged the audience to demand more from those in positions of power. SRK during the 3rd act almost breaks the fourth wall and speaks to his fans, through his art. Some might think this is not what they signed up for, and it's too political however Jawan is urging you to demand excellence from your elected representatives, regardless of their affiliations..

As fas as, action is concerned, not counting the last Vijay-SRK combat scene which was typical Atlee no-attempt-to-hide-cable action scene, this movie boasts action set pieces that could rival Hollywood. One car chase scene gave me vibes of Fast and Furious, while another one of Taken with its fast cuts. Additionally, a border skirmish mission scene is expertly edited with lengthy shots.

Overall, this movie needs to be watched in Big Screen.

Title: Massy - Emotional - Elevation. With a socio-political message.

A hardcore masala entertainer that's sure to stand tall in SRK's filmography... Atlee presents SRK in a massy character and he is 🔥🔥🔥... Move over Pathaan, Jawan is here to conquer hearts and BO, both.

Jawan is loaded with aces... Razor-sharp screenplay, attention-grabbing episodes, splendid action pieces, larger-than-life frames, peppy soundtrack, also the pace and energy never dips... However, it's the clash of the gladiators - SRK and VijaySethupathi - that's the driving force of Jawan.

Jawan is is bolstered by towering performances from the proficient cast... Right from VijaySethupathi to Nayanthara, DeepikaPadukone and SanjayDutt, each actor shines in this well-constructed script.

Having said that, #Jawan rightfully belongs to SRK. It doesn't take a crystal ball to foretell that 2023 belongs to SRK... Now let's hear the ROAR at the BO.

Arnob Ashraful

Jawan is one of the best movie of King Khan for me. It must be the biggest hit of Atlee.

Songs - Awesome song writing. Even as usual so many background dancers have seen, especially in the Zinda banda song.

Direction and writing - No doubt. It is fabulous. Even the screenplay was also great and commendable. The farmer scene very well written. The duplicate rifle scene could have been better. Title: An Action-Packed Thriller with a Powerful Message...
review:
Jawan, directed by Atlee, is a must-watch for fans of Shah Rukh Khan and action-packed thrillers. The movie delves deeper into the corruption of the system and sheds light on some of the hidden facets of our daily lives. With its seamless integration of stunning CGI effects and a thought-provoking message, Jawan succeeds in captivating its audience from the very beginning.

The music, composed by Anirudh Ravichander, perfectly complements the movie's theme and elevates the character of Shah Rukh Khan, who fights against injustice in society and urges people to be wiser in their voting rights in the face of societal challenges.

The supporting cast does an excellent job of helping to shape the story, but it is Nayanthara who steals the show as an opposing force to Shah Rukh Khan's Azad. Their on-screen chemistry adds a captivating layer of complexity to the story, further enhancing the film's intrigue.

The villain, portrayed masterfully by Vijay Sethupathi, adds tension and an element of danger as the protagonist works to overcome obstacles and achieve his goals of encouraging people to look at society without prejudice towards wealth or social class, urging us to perceive all individuals as equal human beings.

Jawan is a movie that skilfully entertains and educates you about different aspects of society, making it a must-watch for all audiences. Whether you're a fan of Shah Rukh Khan or not, the film's action and thrills promise to deliver a gripping experience. Overall, Jawan is an action-packed thriller with a powerful message of social justice that promises to leave a lasting impact.

Title: An Action-Packed Thriller with a Social Conscience
review:
"Jawan" is a dynamic action film that manages to pack a punch while also delving into profound emotional and social issues. The movie maintains a relentless pace, weaving its narrative with impactful action sequences that are heightened by the presence of genuine emotion.

**Balancing Action and Emotion (8/10):**
"Jawan" finds the delicate equilibrium between high-octane action and heartfelt emotion. This unique blend not only entertains but also gives depth to the action sequences, making them more resonant with the audience. It's evident that the filmmakers aimed to create an action film with a heart, and they succeeded in doing so.

**Addressing Social and Political Issues (9/10):**
The film takes a bold step by addressing pressing social and political concerns. What's notable is its impartial approach; "Jawan" isn't politically biased but instead scrutinizes the systemic flaws ingrained in our society. This choice ensures that its message remains universally relevant.

**Pacing and Emotional Depth (7/10):**
While the movie's fast tempo adds to its excitement, it occasionally hampers the emotional depth of certain scenes. Slowing down at times could have allowed for more profound exploration of the characters' feelings and experiences. Nonetheless, the film manages to forge a connection between the audience and its characters.

**Cinematic Brilliance (9/10):**
Visually, "Jawan" is a work of art. The cinematography is nothing short of exceptional, immersing viewers in a visually captivating world. The film's stunning visuals contribute significantly to its overall allure, making it a cinematic treat.

**Music and Score (8/10):**
While the movie's songs may not be its strongest suit, the background music is stellar. It enhances crucial scenes, adding depth and intensity to the storytelling. The score elevates the overall quality of the film.

**Outstanding Performances (9/10):**
Shahrukh Khan delivers a remarkable performance that steals the show. His portrayal is compelling and authentic, adding layers to his character. Vijay Sethupathi's presence is a welcome addition to the ensemble cast, contributing to the film's overall excellence.

**Conclusion (8/10):**
"Jawan" is an action-packed thriller that doesn't forget to carry a social conscience. It successfully navigates the terrain of action and emotion, offering audiences a unique cinematic experience. While pacing issues may hinder emotional connections at times, the film's cinematic brilliance, strong performances, and impactful background score make it a must-watch. "Jawan" not only entertains but also provokes thought on the systemic issues it confronts, making it a compelling addition to the action genre.

Title: Peak entertainment, SRK in his massiest avatar
review:
This is cinema, peak entertainment. Insane theatrical & cinematic experience, got goosebumps all over. I cannot properly explain the spectrum of emotions I went through, want to live in that moment forever. It's wholesome seeing Shah Rukh Khan in this badass massy avatar, literally a dream come true. His screen presence is at an all-time high, he clearly had the time of his life. Will forever be grateful to Atlee for this. This has one of the best interval block of all time & one of the best SRK entries. So many elevation sequences, you will feel the chills. Action sequences packed a big punch and they are only elevated to another level Anirudh's BGM. SRK broke the 4th wall during his monologue to talk to the audience in his unabashedly political avatar and giving his stance, was extremely powerful. The theatres erupted during Zinda Banda & Not Ramaiya Vastavaiya and people started dancing, that was crazy. Even Imax turned into a single screen. It felt so good dancing with other SRK fans.

Deepika in her limited screen time left a big impact. The way she emoted I felt her pain and she made me very emotional. Nayanthara has that aura & power and I understand why they call her lady superstar. They both were stunning. Vijay Sethupathi was very menacing. Other cast members did really well too, the girl gang were on fire. Some of the scenes made me cry, no kidding. The emotions hit right on point.

To sum it all up - Anirudh did his thing, Atlee cooked his magic & SRK in his charismatic & electrifying best. Add cool characters & cast, emotions, revenge, mass action & elevation sequences, powerful music, strong social message, colourful visuals and we get this beautiful entertainment package called Jawan that captivates audiences from start to finish with proper blend of masala elements. It's a treat for SRK fans & neutrals alike.

After watching thrice already, I can definitely say it has an insane repeat value and gets better & clearer with each rewatch. Will watch again. Audience is in for a nonstop cheers, whistles, claps, shouts, cheering & hooting ride, I am still having a sore throat from all that. It's not a film, it's an emotion that will make you smile and give a heartwarming feeling.

Title: Double the Shah Rukh, Double the Thrills: 'Jawan' unleashes SRK's insane stardom 💥 💥
review:
In the mesmerizing world of Indian cinema, "Jawan" emerges as a true gem! Shah Rukh Khan's unparalleled charisma, breathtaking action sequences, emotional moments, twists and turns, and the magic of Atlee all shine brightly in the film.

{The story begins with Azad Rathore, played with flair by Shah Rukh Khan, orchestrating a gripping hostage situation aboard a Mumbai Metro. With a formidable gang of inmates turned co-conspirators, Azad and his team take us on a heart-pounding journey. His negotiation with officer Narmada Rai, portrayed by Nayanthara, is a battle of wits that keeps you on the edge of your seat. 'Jawan' delves deep into the human spirit and social conscience. Azad's daring plan to demand a colossal sum from the government unfolds with a hidden purpose, to uplift the underprivileged.}

The film is a perfect masala entertainer with all the elements of a good action thriller: high-octane action sequences, suspenseful plot twists, and emotional moments. What makes 'Jawan' even more riveting is SRK's remarkable double role as both Azad and Vikram, and he is ably supported by Nayanthara, Vijay Sethupathi, and Deepika Padukone. The supporting cast is also excellent.

The action sequences in the film are some of the best that I have seen in a Hindi film in recent years. They are well-choreographed and exciting, and they keep the audience on the edge of their seats. The plot twists are also well-executed, and they keep the audience guessing until the very end. The film also has a strong emotional core, and it deals with some important social issues such as corruption, injustice, and environmental pollution. The film does not shy away from tackling these issues head-on, and it offers a powerful message of hope and redemption. The film also draws inspiration from the popular web series "Money Heist," seamlessly blending moments into the story. The past and present narratives converge brilliantly, unraveling a tale of heroism, redemption, and the pursuit of justice.

Here are some of the things that I particularly liked about the film:

Obviously Shah Rukh Khan!

Seeing Nayantara in action.

The action sequences are top-notch.

The plot twists are well-executed.

The film has a strong emotional core.

The performances by the cast are excellent.

The film deals with important social issues.

I would definitely recommend 'Jawan' to anyone who enjoys action thrillers. It is a well-made film with something to offer everyone.

Title: Junk, trash of a story and bad editing
review: I just could not believe my eyes when i was 30 mins into this piece of trash. How many times was this story done in the 1989s in Hindi as well as South Indian movies? Why is it being repeated shamelessly in 2023? Just because it's SRK movie? Seriously? Then, another major concern is that if this is the future of Tamil-Hindi cinema collaboration, I am happy with watching selected Hindi movies only. Let me explain. Typically Tamil action movies with superstars(like Rajnikant) have each and every action scene either in slow motion or all the vehicles are running in 1.5 times to 2 times the speed, same for bikes! Doesn't stop there, it is exactly the same with dialogues, the last words of a sentence are not over and the editor keeps switching to the other person eith such a speed that even in delicate romantic scenes there is no delicacy. Till the end, it was like watching an anime movie with no respect for a cinema viewer's sensibility. No, this movie is an action movie you will say, but the rhythm of the action, dialigues and Editing is something that probably the Tamil audience is used to watching and I respect that fact. Just that the editing and rhythm is totally different from that of Hindi movies, and Hindi movie viewers are used to a certain rhythm of dialogues, movements, a certain gap between dialogues, etc etc. This Hindi movie directed by a Tamil director feels like you are watching a Tamil movie dubbed in Hindi, in each and every scene!! Throughout you get a feel you are not watching a Hindi movie at all, something is alien, something is missing. All the songs except for the end title one. Do not sound like Hindi movie songs either. If this is how the future of Tamil Hindi movies collaboration looks like, please give us a Hindi movie directed by a Hindi language understanding director only, please please!! This movie was a torture to watch. Hope you understand Hindi movie producers!! Thankyou!!

Title: Few issues aside, this one's a great commercial flick!
review:
So, I just came from my show of Jawan, and this was an exhilarating three hours, and I have tons to discuss.

Since all my theatre viewings have incorporated a different review format (detracting from my analytical perspective), I will make no exception here. I will put forth the positives and negatives like I did on all occasions.

Without further ado, let's begin and let me first start with what did I like.

Positives:-

Technicalities - I cannot stress enough how rich and gorgeous the film looks. Each and every shot was infused with fitting colours and contrast.

Shah Rukh Khan - We have never seen Shah Rukh Khan in such a massy avatar before. What Atlee Kumar had done with him here is exceptional, and Bollywood directors should take notes seriously. Kudos to SRK, too, as he had completely surrendered himself to the director, putting faith in his vision.

Emotional Core - Those who have seen Atlee's earlier works know how much importance he generally gives to the emotional aspects of his stories, and "Jawan" is nothing different. You will shed some tears.

Background Music - Anirudh had yet again shown his true worth. What a terrific composition. It had uplifted and exacerbated every sequence! It's one of his best, for sure.

Yeah, that's about it. Now, I am moving on to the things I did not like or had qualms about.

Negatives:-

Third Act and its mishaps - The concluding Act, or the last thirty minutes to be precise, came off as volatile and incoherent compared to the rest of the script. It seems like Atlee had made some last-minute changes or was in a great hurry to finish off the screenplay and, in doing so, ineluctably messed up the inherent flow of the narrative.

Tonal Misbalance - It might sound nitpicky, and maybe I'm doing just that, but embedding comedy in serious moments didn't come across as a wise call. It took away the intensity and made the situations less intimidating.

Lack of Material for the Supporting Cast - I knew the supporting actors would have little to do here, and my assumptions were proved correct. Nayanthara, Sunil Grover, The Six Girls: Although they did whatever was demanded of them, their characters leave a whole lot to be desired. There were opportunities to make them shine and come out of their shells, but Atlee didn't; it was deliberate from his end.

Now, it's time for those things I have mixed feelings on.

Mixed:-

Cameos / Guest Appearances - There are two special appearances in the movie (No, Thalapathy Vijay is not there if you are wondering); one from Deepika and the other one comes from a Veteran in the business (not going to name him though, you will see it for yourself).

Now, I absolutely adored Deepika here. She was given about twenty minutes of screen time, and she gave it all and made her part so impactful that you'll remember her after the film finishes. Sadly, I cannot say the same for the "other" cameo, which felt more like a last-minute touch-up or an input from the Producers' end rather than an indispensable part of Atlee's pre-planned vision.

Messaging - Each and Every message in the film bears significance and the topics they speak about need to be brought up in our day-to-day conversations. Having said that, I did not like the way those messages were delivered, especially towards the end when SRK breaks the fourth wall and speaks directly to the audience. It was fine at the beginning but became so preachy by the end!

To summarise my train of thought, I had a very good experience overall, and the community viewing aspect definitely played its part. Despite a couple of issues, I suggest everybody go and witness this spectacle of a feature on the biggest screen you could afford to go to. If you love SRK, it's a must-watch no-brainer; even if you do not consider yourself a fan, go for the exhilaration. Trust me, you won't have much to complain about.

Title: Basically A Tamil mass film starring SRK
review:
Jawan may be shot mainly in Hindi, but it is a Tamil mass film in all its essence. Atlee is known for basically copying ideas from other famous movies and making them in his own style for his favourite star. Jawan is an amalgamation of many such movies, including his own films. But the core is the man against system idea which was popularized in this particular form by director Shankar and since then it has been embraced by South Indian movies. The first half had a few interesting stuff going on but it was kinda boring me. I've heard about how Atlee dismisses normal logic from how people talk about his films like Bigil which is even more apparent here. Knowing this and being an SRK fan, I was expecting stuff in the film that defies logic. Overall it didn't affect me too much but the first half had a lot of such things that continuously happened that made it hard to enjoy it. Not using logic to be entertained is fine but the film has too much story in it and most of it doesn't have any logic. It's following too many formula-driven story ideas at once without properly integrating them. But once the interval block action sequence started the movie turned around for me. From there onwards it was a very entertaining movie and if you can watch it with a great audience it will be a great experience.

But it is to be noted that, there's so much story here, but no real characters. The characters are not at all written with depth but everyone is given certain visual flairs to make it very appealing. Nayanthara's character felt so weak but the way she is presented visually through the action scenes kind of makes you forget that.

Some dialogues and scenes kind of felt like a personal message from SRK which seamlessly blends with this kind of film. Maybe that's why he wanted to do such a south-style film.

All in all, I thought it was an average to okay film because I am an SRK fan and enjoyed watching his action scenes and song sequences in the theatre.

If Die Hard is a Christmas movie, this is an Onam movie 😇
